1→import 'dart:ui';
     2→
     3→import 'package:flutter/foundation.dart';
     4→import 'package:obers_ui/src/foundation/theme/oi_animation_config.dart';
     5→import 'package:obers_ui/src/foundation/theme/oi_color_scheme.dart';
     6→import 'package:obers_ui/src/foundation/theme/oi_color_swatch.dart';
     7→import 'package:obers_ui/src/foundation/theme/oi_component_themes.dart';
     8→import 'package:obers_ui/src/foundation/theme/oi_decoration_theme.dart';
     9→import 'package:obers_ui/src/foundation/theme/oi_effects_theme.dart';
    10→import 'package:obers_ui/src/foundation/theme/oi_radius_scale.dart';
    11→import 'package:obers_ui/src/foundation/theme/oi_shadow_scale.dart';
    12→import 'package:obers_ui/src/foundation/theme/oi_spacing_scale.dart';
    13→import 'package:obers_ui/src/foundation/theme/oi_text_theme.dart';
    14→
    15→/// The master theme container for the obers_ui design system.
    16→///
    17→/// [OiThemeData] aggregates all design tokens — colors, typography, spacing,
    18→/// radii, shadows, animations, effects, and per-component overrides — into a
    19→/// single immutable object.
    20→///
    21→/// Inject it into the widget tree via [OiApp] or [OiTheme].
    22→///
    23→/// {@category Foundation}
    24→@immutable
    25→class OiThemeData {
    26→  /// Creates an [OiThemeData] with explicit values for all design tokens.
    27→  const OiThemeData({
    28→    required this.brightness,
    29→    required this.colors,
    30→    required this.textTheme,
    31→    required this.spacing,
    32→    required this.radius,
    33→    required this.shadows,
    34→    required this.animations,
    35→    required this.effects,
    36→    required this.decoration,
    37→    required this.components,
    38→    this.fontFamily,
    39→    this.monoFontFamily,
    40→  });
    41→
    42→  /// Creates the standard light theme.
    43→  factory OiThemeData.light({
    44→    String? fontFamily,
    45→    String? monoFontFamily,
    46→    OiRadiusPreference radiusPreference = OiRadiusPreference.medium,
    47→    OiComponentThemes? components,
    48→  }) {
    49→    final colors = OiColorScheme.light();
    50→    return OiThemeData(
    51→      brightness: Brightness.light,
    52→      colors: colors,
    53→      textTheme: OiTextTheme.standard(
    54→        fontFamily: fontFamily,
    55→        monoFontFamily: monoFontFamily,
    56→      ),
    57→      spacing: OiSpacingScale.standard(),
    58→      radius: OiRadiusScale.forPreference(radiusPreference),
    59→      shadows: OiShadowScale.standard(),
    60→      animations: const OiAnimationConfig.standard(),
    61→      effects: OiEffectsTheme.standard(primaryColor: colors.primary.base),
    62→      decoration: OiDecorationTheme.standard(
    63→        primaryColor: colors.primary.base,
    64→        errorColor: colors.error.base,
    65→      ),
    66→      components: components ?? const OiComponentThemes.empty(),
    67→      fontFamily: fontFamily,
    68→      monoFontFamily: monoFontFamily,
    69→    );
    70→  }
    71→
    72→  /// Creates the standard dark theme.
    73→  factory OiThemeData.dark({
    74→    String? fontFamily,
    75→    String? monoFontFamily,
    76→    OiRadiusPreference radiusPreference = OiRadiusPreference.medium,
    77→    OiComponentThemes? components,
    78→  }) {
    79→    final colors = OiColorScheme.dark();
    80→    return OiThemeData(
    81→      brightness: Brightness.dark,
    82→      colors: colors,
    83→      textTheme: OiTextTheme.standard(
    84→        fontFamily: fontFamily,
    85→        monoFontFamily: monoFontFamily,
    86→      ),
    87→      spacing: OiSpacingScale.standard(),
    88→      radius: OiRadiusScale.forPreference(radiusPreference),
    89→      shadows: OiShadowScale.dark(),
    90→      animations: const OiAnimationConfig.standard(),
    91→      effects: OiEffectsTheme.standard(primaryColor: colors.primary.base),
    92→      decoration: OiDecorationTheme.standard(
    93→        primaryColor: colors.primary.base,
    94→        errorColor: colors.error.base,
    95→      ),
    96→      components: components ?? const OiComponentThemes.empty(),
    97→      fontFamily: fontFamily,
    98→      monoFontFamily: monoFontFamily,
    99→    );
   100→  }
   101→
   102→  /// Creates a theme derived from a brand color.
   103→  ///
   104→  /// The brand [color] is used as the primary swatch, and all other
   105→  /// semantic colors are derived automatically.
   106→  factory OiThemeData.fromBrand({
   107→    required Color color,
   108→    Brightness brightness = Brightness.light,
   109→    String? fontFamily,
   110→    String? monoFontFamily,
   111→    OiRadiusPreference radiusPreference = OiRadiusPreference.medium,
   112→    OiComponentThemes? components,
   113→  }) {
   114→    final base = brightness == Brightness.light
   115→        ? OiThemeData.light(
   116→            fontFamily: fontFamily,
   117→            monoFontFamily: monoFontFamily,
   118→            radiusPreference: radiusPreference,
   119→            components: components,
   120→          )
   121→        : OiThemeData.dark(
   122→            fontFamily: fontFamily,
   123→            monoFontFamily: monoFontFamily,
   124→            radiusPreference: radiusPreference,
   125→            components: components,
   126→          );
   127→
   128→    // Override primary color with the brand color
   129→    final brandColors = base.colors.copyWith(
   130→      primary: OiColorSwatch.from(color),
   131→      borderFocus: color,
   132→    );
   133→
   134→    return base.copyWith(
   135→      colors: brandColors,
   136→      effects: OiEffectsTheme.standard(primaryColor: color),
   137→      decoration: OiDecorationTheme.standard(
   138→        primaryColor: color,
   139→        errorColor: base.colors.error.base,
   140→      ),
   141→    );
   142→  }
   143→
   144→  /// Whether this is a light or dark theme.
   145→  final Brightness brightness;
   146→
   147→  /// The color scheme providing all semantic color tokens.
   148→  final OiColorScheme colors;
   149→
   150→  /// The typography scale.
   151→  final OiTextTheme textTheme;
   152→
   153→  /// The spacing scale.
   154→  final OiSpacingScale spacing;
   155→
   156→  /// The border radius scale.
   157→  final OiRadiusScale radius;
   158→
   159→  /// The box shadow scale.
   160→  final OiShadowScale shadows;
   161→
   162→  /// The animation duration configuration.
   163→  final OiAnimationConfig animations;
   164→
   165→  /// The interactive state effects (hover, focus, active, etc.).
   166→  final OiEffectsTheme effects;
   167→
   168→  /// The decoration theme (borders, gradients).
   169→  final OiDecorationTheme decoration;
   170→
   171→  /// Per-component theme overrides.
   172→  final OiComponentThemes components;
   173→
   174→  /// Optional font family override for text styles.
   175→  final String? fontFamily;
   176→
   177→  /// Optional monospace font family for code text.
   178→  final String? monoFontFamily;
   179→
   180→  /// Whether this theme has dark brightness.
   181→  bool get isDark => brightness == Brightness.dark;
   182→
   183→  /// Whether this theme has light brightness.
   184→  bool get isLight => brightness == Brightness.light;
   185→
   186→  /// Creates a copy with optionally overridden fields.
   187→  OiThemeData copyWith({
   188→    Brightness? brightness,
   189→    OiColorScheme? colors,
   190→    OiTextTheme? textTheme,
   191→    OiSpacingScale? spacing,
   192→    OiRadiusScale? radius,
   193→    OiShadowScale? shadows,
   194→    OiAnimationConfig? animations,
   195→    OiEffectsTheme? effects,
   196→    OiDecorationTheme? decoration,
   197→    OiComponentThemes? components,
   198→    String? fontFamily,
   199→    String? monoFontFamily,
   200→  }) {
   201→    return OiThemeData(
   202→      brightness: brightness ?? this.brightness,
   203→      colors: colors ?? this.colors,
   204→      textTheme: textTheme ?? this.textTheme,
   205→      spacing: spacing ?? this.spacing,
   206→      radius: radius ?? this.radius,
   207→      shadows: shadows ?? this.shadows,
   208→      animations: animations ?? this.animations,
   209→      effects: effects ?? this.effects,
   210→      decoration: decoration ?? this.decoration,
   211→      components: components ?? this.components,
   212→      fontFamily: fontFamily ?? this.fontFamily,
   213→      monoFontFamily: monoFontFamily ?? this.monoFontFamily,
   214→    );
   215→  }
   216→
   217→  /// Merges [other] into this theme, with [other]'s non-null values taking
   218→  /// precedence.
   219→  OiThemeData merge(OiThemeData other) {
   220→    return copyWith(
   221→      brightness: other.brightness,
   222→      colors: other.colors,
   223→      textTheme: other.textTheme,
   224→      spacing: other.spacing,
   225→      radius: other.radius,
   226→      shadows: other.shadows,
   227→      animations: other.animations,
   228→      effects: other.effects,
   229→      decoration: other.decoration,
   230→      components: other.components,
   231→      fontFamily: other.fontFamily,
   232→      monoFontFamily: other.monoFontFamily,
   233→    );
   234→  }
   235→
   236→  /// Linearly interpolates between two [OiThemeData] instances.
   237→  // lerp is a static method by Flutter convention, not a constructor.
   238→  // ignore: prefer_constructors_over_static_methods
   239→  static OiThemeData lerp(OiThemeData a, OiThemeData b, double t) {
   240→    if (t == 0) return a;
   241→    if (t == 1) return b;
   242→    return OiThemeData(
   243→      brightness: t < 0.5 ? a.brightness : b.brightness,
   244→      colors: OiColorScheme.lerp(a.colors, b.colors, t),
   245→      textTheme: OiTextTheme.lerp(a.textTheme, b.textTheme, t),
   246→      spacing: t < 0.5 ? a.spacing : b.spacing,
   247→      radius: t < 0.5 ? a.radius : b.radius,
   248→      shadows: t < 0.5 ? a.shadows : b.shadows,
   249→      animations: t < 0.5 ? a.animations : b.animations,
   250→      effects: t < 0.5 ? a.effects : b.effects,
   251→      decoration: t < 0.5 ? a.decoration : b.decoration,
   252→      components: t < 0.5 ? a.components : b.components,
   253→      fontFamily: t < 0.5 ? a.fontFamily : b.fontFamily,
   254→      monoFontFamily: t < 0.5 ? a.monoFontFamily : b.monoFontFamily,
   255→    );
   256→  }
   257→
   258→  @override
   259→  bool operator ==(Object other) {
   260→    if (identical(this, other)) return true;
   261→    return other is OiThemeData &&
   262→        other.brightness == brightness &&
   263→        other.colors == colors &&
   264→        other.textTheme == textTheme &&
   265→        other.spacing == spacing &&
   266→        other.radius == radius &&
   267→        other.shadows == shadows &&
   268→        other.animations == animations &&
   269→        other.effects == effects &&
   270→        other.decoration == decoration &&
   271→        other.components == components &&
   272→        other.fontFamily == fontFamily &&
   273→        other.monoFontFamily == monoFontFamily;
   274→  }
   275→
   276→  @override
   277→  int get hashCode => Object.hashAll([
   278→    brightness,
   279→    colors,
   280→    textTheme,
   281→    spacing,
   282→    radius,
   283→    shadows,
   284→    animations,
   285→    effects,
   286→    decoration,
   287→    components,
   288→    fontFamily,
   289→    monoFontFamily,
   290→  ]);
   291→}
   292→

     4→import 'package:obers_ui/src/foundation/theme/oi_color_swatch.dart';
     5→
     6→/// A complete semantic color palette for the Obers UI design system.
     7→///
     8→/// [OiColorScheme] holds all color tokens used by Obers UI widgets — semantic
     9→/// swatches (primary, accent, success, warning, error, info), surface / overlay
    10→/// colors, text colors, border colors, glass-effect colors, and a categorical
    11→/// chart palette.
    12→///
    13→/// Two built-in palettes are provided via factory constructors:
    14→/// - [OiColorScheme.light] — a light-background palette.
    15→/// - [OiColorScheme.dark] — a dark-background palette.
    16→///
    17→/// Instances are immutable; use [copyWith] to derive a modified copy or
    18→/// [OiColorScheme.lerp] to animate between two schemes.
    19→///
    20→/// {@category Foundation}
    21→@immutable
    22→class OiColorScheme {
    23→  /// Creates an [OiColorScheme] with all color fields specified explicitly.
    24→  const OiColorScheme({
    25→    required this.primary,
    26→    required this.accent,
    27→    required this.success,
    28→    required this.warning,
    29→    required this.error,
    30→    required this.info,
    31→    required this.background,
    32→    required this.surface,
    33→    required this.surfaceHover,
    34→    required this.surfaceActive,
    35→    required this.surfaceSubtle,
    36→    required this.overlay,
    37→    required this.text,
    38→    required this.textSubtle,
    39→    required this.textMuted,
    40→    required this.textInverse,
    41→    required this.textOnPrimary,
    42→    required this.border,
    43→    required this.borderSubtle,
    44→    required this.borderFocus,
    45→    required this.borderError,
    46→    required this.glassBackground,
    47→    required this.glassBorder,
    48→    required this.chart,
    49→  });
    50→
    51→  /// A light-background color scheme.
    52→  ///
    53→  /// Uses a blue primary, teal accent, and light grey surfaces.
    54→  factory OiColorScheme.light() {
    55→    return OiColorScheme(
    56→      primary: OiColorSwatch.from(const Color(0xFF2563EB)),
    57→      accent: OiColorSwatch.from(const Color(0xFF0D9488)),
    58→      success: OiColorSwatch.from(const Color(0xFF16A34A)),
    59→      warning: OiColorSwatch.from(const Color(0xFFD97706)),
    60→      error: OiColorSwatch.from(const Color(0xFFDC2626)),
    61→      info: OiColorSwatch.from(const Color(0xFF0284C7)),
    62→      background: const Color(0xFFF9FAFB),
    63→      surface: const Color(0xFFFFFFFF),
    64→      surfaceHover: const Color(0xFFF3F4F6),
    65→      surfaceActive: const Color(0xFFE5E7EB),
    66→      surfaceSubtle: const Color(0xFFF9FAFB),
    67→      overlay: const Color(0x66000000),
    68→      text: const Color(0xFF111827),
    69→      textSubtle: const Color(0xFF374151),
    70→      textMuted: const Color(0xFF6B7280),
    71→      textInverse: const Color(0xFFFFFFFF),
    72→      textOnPrimary: const Color(0xFFFFFFFF),
    73→      border: const Color(0xFFD1D5DB),
    74→      borderSubtle: const Color(0xFFE5E7EB),
    75→      borderFocus: const Color(0xFF2563EB),
    76→      borderError: const Color(0xFFDC2626),
    77→      glassBackground: const Color(0x99FFFFFF),
    78→      glassBorder: const Color(0x33FFFFFF),
    79→      chart: const [
    80→        Color(0xFF2563EB),
    81→        Color(0xFF0D9488),
    82→        Color(0xFFD97706),
    83→        Color(0xFFDC2626),
    84→        Color(0xFF7C3AED),
    85→        Color(0xFF0284C7),
    86→        Color(0xFF16A34A),
    87→        Color(0xFFDB2777),
    88→      ],
    89→    );
    90→  }
    91→
    92→  /// A dark-background color scheme.
    93→  ///
    94→  /// Uses a blue primary, teal accent, and near-black surfaces.
    95→  factory OiColorScheme.dark() {
    96→    return OiColorScheme(
    97→      primary: OiColorSwatch.from(const Color(0xFF3B82F6)),
    98→      accent: OiColorSwatch.from(const Color(0xFF14B8A6)),
    99→      success: OiColorSwatch.from(const Color(0xFF22C55E)),
   100→      warning: OiColorSwatch.from(const Color(0xFFF59E0B)),
   101→      error: OiColorSwatch.from(const Color(0xFFEF4444)),
   102→      info: OiColorSwatch.from(const Color(0xFF38BDF8)),
   103→      background: const Color(0xFF0A0A0F),
   104→      surface: const Color(0xFF111118),
   105→      surfaceHover: const Color(0xFF1C1C26),
   106→      surfaceActive: const Color(0xFF252533),
   107→      surfaceSubtle: const Color(0xFF0D0D14),
   108→      overlay: const Color(0x99000000),
   109→      text: const Color(0xFFF9FAFB),
   110→      textSubtle: const Color(0xFFD1D5DB),
   111→      textMuted: const Color(0xFF9CA3AF),
   112→      textInverse: const Color(0xFF111827),
   113→      textOnPrimary: const Color(0xFFFFFFFF),
   114→      border: const Color(0xFF374151),
   115→      borderSubtle: const Color(0xFF1F2937),
   116→      borderFocus: const Color(0xFF3B82F6),
   117→      borderError: const Color(0xFFEF4444),
   118→      glassBackground: const Color(0x33FFFFFF),
   119→      glassBorder: const Color(0x1AFFFFFF),
   120→      chart: const [
   121→        Color(0xFF3B82F6),
   122→        Color(0xFF14B8A6),
   123→        Color(0xFFF59E0B),
   124→        Color(0xFFEF4444),
   125→        Color(0xFF8B5CF6),
   126→        Color(0xFF38BDF8),
   127→        Color(0xFF22C55E),
   128→        Color(0xFFF472B6),
   129→      ],
   130→    );
   131→  }

   133→  /// Linearly interpolates between two [OiColorScheme]s.
   134→  ///
   135→  /// [t] must be in the range [0, 1]. At [t] = 0 the result equals [a];
   136→  /// at [t] = 1 the result equals [b].
   137→  OiColorScheme.lerp(OiColorScheme a, OiColorScheme b, double t)
   138→    : primary = OiColorSwatch.lerp(a.primary, b.primary, t),
   139→      accent = OiColorSwatch.lerp(a.accent, b.accent, t),
   140→      success = OiColorSwatch.lerp(a.success, b.success, t),
   141→      warning = OiColorSwatch.lerp(a.warning, b.warning, t),
   142→      error = OiColorSwatch.lerp(a.error, b.error, t),
   143→      info = OiColorSwatch.lerp(a.info, b.info, t),
   144→      background = Color.lerp(a.background, b.background, t)!,
   145→      surface = Color.lerp(a.surface, b.surface, t)!,
   146→      surfaceHover = Color.lerp(a.surfaceHover, b.surfaceHover, t)!,
   147→      surfaceActive = Color.lerp(a.surfaceActive, b.surfaceActive, t)!,
   148→      surfaceSubtle = Color.lerp(a.surfaceSubtle, b.surfaceSubtle, t)!,
   149→      overlay = Color.lerp(a.overlay, b.overlay, t)!,
   150→      text = Color.lerp(a.text, b.text, t)!,
   151→      textSubtle = Color.lerp(a.textSubtle, b.textSubtle, t)!,
   152→      textMuted = Color.lerp(a.textMuted, b.textMuted, t)!,
   153→      textInverse = Color.lerp(a.textInverse, b.textInverse, t)!,
   154→      textOnPrimary = Color.lerp(a.textOnPrimary, b.textOnPrimary, t)!,
   155→      border = Color.lerp(a.border, b.border, t)!,
   156→      borderSubtle = Color.lerp(a.borderSubtle, b.borderSubtle, t)!,
   157→      borderFocus = Color.lerp(a.borderFocus, b.borderFocus, t)!,
   158→      borderError = Color.lerp(a.borderError, b.borderError, t)!,
   159→      glassBackground = Color.lerp(a.glassBackground, b.glassBackground, t)!,
   160→      glassBorder = Color.lerp(a.glassBorder, b.glassBorder, t)!,
   161→      chart = List<Color>.generate(
   162→        a.chart.length,
   163→        (i) => Color.lerp(
   164→          a.chart[i],
   165→          b.chart[i < b.chart.length ? i : b.chart.length - 1],
   166→          t,
   167→        )!,
   168→      );
   169→
   170→  // ── Semantic swatches ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   171→
   172→  /// The primary brand color swatch (base, light, dark, muted, foreground).
   173→  final OiColorSwatch primary;
   174→
   175→  /// The accent / secondary brand color swatch.
   176→  final OiColorSwatch accent;
   177→
   178→  /// The success / positive state color swatch.
   179→  final OiColorSwatch success;
   180→
   181→  /// The warning / caution state color swatch.
   182→  final OiColorSwatch warning;
   183→
   184→  /// The error / destructive state color swatch.
   185→  final OiColorSwatch error;
   186→
   187→  /// The informational state color swatch.
   188→  final OiColorSwatch info;
   189→
   190→  // ── Background / surface ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
   191→
   192→  /// The app-level page background color.
   193→  final Color background;
   194→
   195→  /// The default card / panel surface color.
   196→  final Color surface;
   197→
   198→  /// Surface color for hover states.
   199→  final Color surfaceHover;
   200→
   201→  /// Surface color for active / pressed states.
   202→  final Color surfaceActive;
   203→
   204→  /// A very subtle surface tint, useful for zebra-striped rows or sidebars.
   205→  final Color surfaceSubtle;
   206→
   207→  /// Semi-transparent overlay used for modals and drawers.
   208→  final Color overlay;
   209→
   210→  // ── Text ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   211→
   212→  /// Primary text color (highest contrast).
   213→  final Color text;
   214→
   215→  /// Secondary / subdued text color.
   216→  final Color textSubtle;
   217→
   218→  /// Tertiary / muted text color (placeholders, captions).
   219→  final Color textMuted;
   220→
   221→  /// Inverse text color, intended for use on dark surfaces in a light scheme
   222→  /// and vice-versa.
   223→  final Color textInverse;
   224→
   225→  /// Text color for content placed directly on the [primary] swatch base.
   226→  final Color textOnPrimary;
   227→
   228→  // ── Borders ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   229→
   230→  /// Default border / divider color.
   231→  final Color border;
   232→
   233→  /// Subtle border color, lighter than [border].
   234→  final Color borderSubtle;
   235→
   236→  /// Border color used to indicate keyboard focus.
   237→  final Color borderFocus;
   238→
   239→  /// Border color used to indicate a validation error.
   240→  final Color borderError;
   241→
   242→  // ── Glass / frosted ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   243→
   244→  /// Semi-transparent background for glass / frosted-glass effect surfaces.
   245→  final Color glassBackground;
   246→
   247→  /// Semi-transparent border for glass / frosted-glass effect surfaces.
   248→  final Color glassBorder;
   249→
   250→  // ── Chart palette ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   251→
   252→  /// An ordered list of 8 categorical colors for charts and data visualization.
   253→  final List<Color> chart;
   254→
   255→  // ── Utilities ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   256→
   257→  /// Creates a copy of this scheme with the specified fields replaced.
   258→  OiColorScheme copyWith({
   259→    OiColorSwatch? primary,
   260→    OiColorSwatch? accent,
   261→    OiColorSwatch? success,
   262→    OiColorSwatch? warning,
   263→    OiColorSwatch? error,
   264→    OiColorSwatch? info,
   265→    Color? background,
   266→    Color? surface,
   267→    Color? surfaceHover,
   268→    Color? surfaceActive,
   269→    Color? surfaceSubtle,
   270→    Color? overlay,
   271→    Color? text,
   272→    Color? textSubtle,
   273→    Color? textMuted,
   274→    Color? textInverse,
   275→    Color? textOnPrimary,
   276→    Color? border,
   277→    Color? borderSubtle,
   278→    Color? borderFocus,
   279→    Color? borderError,
   280→    Color? glassBackground,
   281→    Color? glassBorder,
   282→    List<Color>? chart,
   283→  }) {
   284→    return OiColorScheme(
   285→      primary: primary ?? this.primary,
   286→      accent: accent ?? this.accent,
   287→      success: success ?? this.success,
   288→      warning: warning ?? this.warning,
   289→      error: error ?? this.error,
   290→      info: info ?? this.info,
   291→      background: background ?? this.background,
   292→      surface: surface ?? this.surface,
   293→      surfaceHover: surfaceHover ?? this.surfaceHover,
   294→      surfaceActive: surfaceActive ?? this.surfaceActive,
   295→      surfaceSubtle: surfaceSubtle ?? this.surfaceSubtle,
   296→      overlay: overlay ?? this.overlay,
   297→      text: text ?? this.text,
   298→      textSubtle: textSubtle ?? this.textSubtle,
   299→      textMuted: textMuted ?? this.textMuted,
   300→      textInverse: textInverse ?? this.textInverse,
   301→      textOnPrimary: textOnPrimary ?? this.textOnPrimary,
   302→      border: border ?? this.border,
   303→      borderSubtle: borderSubtle ?? this.borderSubtle,
   304→      borderFocus: borderFocus ?? this.borderFocus,
   305→      borderError: borderError ?? this.borderError,
   306→      glassBackground: glassBackground ?? this.glassBackground,
   307→      glassBorder: glassBorder ?? this.glassBorder,
   308→      chart: chart ?? this.chart,
   309→    );
   310→  }
   311→
   312→  @override
   313→  bool operator ==(Object other) {
   314→    if (identical(this, other)) return true;
   315→    if (other is! OiColorScheme) return false;
   316→    if (chart.length != other.chart.length) return false;
   317→    for (var i = 0; i < chart.length; i++) {
   318→      if (chart[i] != other.chart[i]) return false;
   319→    }
   320→    return other.primary == primary &&
   321→        other.accent == accent &&
   322→        other.success == success &&
   323→        other.warning == warning &&
   324→        other.error == error &&
   325→        other.info == info &&
   326→        other.background == background &&
   327→        other.surface == surface &&
   328→        other.surfaceHover == surfaceHover &&
   329→        other.surfaceActive == surfaceActive &&
   330→        other.surfaceSubtle == surfaceSubtle &&
   331→        other.overlay == overlay &&
   332→        other.text == text &&
   333→        other.textSubtle == textSubtle &&
   334→        other.textMuted == textMuted &&
   335→        other.textInverse == textInverse &&
   336→        other.textOnPrimary == textOnPrimary &&
   337→        other.border == border &&
   338→        other.borderSubtle == borderSubtle &&
   339→        other.borderFocus == borderFocus &&
   340→        other.borderError == borderError &&
   341→        other.glassBackground == glassBackground &&
   342→        other.glassBorder == glassBorder;
   343→  }
   344→
   345→  @override
   346→  int get hashCode => Object.hashAll([
   347→    primary,
   348→    accent,
   349→    success,
   350→    warning,
   351→    error,
   352→    info,
   353→    background,
   354→    surface,
   355→    surfaceHover,
   356→    surfaceActive,
   357→    surfaceSubtle,
   358→    overlay,
   359→    text,
   360→    textSubtle,
   361→    textMuted,
   362→    textInverse,
   363→    textOnPrimary,
   364→    border,
   365→    borderSubtle,
   366→    borderFocus,
   367→    borderError,
   368→    glassBackground,
   369→    glassBorder,
   370→    ...chart,
   371→  ]);
   372→
   373→  @override
   374→  String toString() =>
   375→      'OiColorScheme(background: $background, '
   376→      'surface: $surface, primary: $primary)';
   377→}

     4→/// Semantic text-style variants used throughout the Obers UI design system.
     5→///
     6→/// Each value corresponds to a named [TextStyle] on [OiTextTheme].
     7→///
     8→/// {@category Foundation}
     9→enum OiLabelVariant {
    10→  /// Hero / marketing display text — largest size.
    11→  display,
    12→
    13→  /// Top-level heading.
    14→  h1,
    15→
    16→  /// Second-level heading.
    17→  h2,
    18→
    19→  /// Third-level heading.
    20→  h3,
    21→
    22→  /// Fourth-level heading.
    23→  h4,
    24→
    25→  /// Standard paragraph body text.
    26→  body,
    27→
    28→  /// Bold / emphasized paragraph body text.
    29→  bodyStrong,
    30→
    31→  /// Small body text, suitable for secondary information.
    32→  small,
    33→
    34→  /// Bold small body text.
    35→  smallStrong,
    36→
    37→  /// Extra-small text, suitable for dense UI elements.
    38→  tiny,
    39→
    40→  /// Caption text below images or media.
    41→  caption,
    42→
    43→  /// Monospace code / preformatted text.
    44→  code,
    45→
    46→  /// All-caps label with letter-spacing, used above sections.
    47→  overline,
    48→
    49→  /// Inline hyperlink text.
    50→  link,
    51→}
    52→
    53→/// A complete set of [TextStyle]s for the Obers UI design system.
    54→///
    55→/// Use [OiTextTheme.standard] to obtain the default type scale, optionally
    56→/// customising the font families. Use [copyWith] to override individual
    57→/// styles, [OiTextTheme.lerp] to animate between two themes, and
    58→/// [styleFor] to look up a style by its [OiLabelVariant] token.
    59→///
    60→/// {@category Foundation}
    61→@immutable
    62→class OiTextTheme {
    63→  /// Creates an [OiTextTheme] with all styles specified explicitly.
    64→  const OiTextTheme({
    65→    required this.display,
    66→    required this.h1,
    67→    required this.h2,
    68→    required this.h3,
    69→    required this.h4,
    70→    required this.body,
    71→    required this.bodyStrong,
    72→    required this.small,
    73→    required this.smallStrong,
    74→    required this.tiny,
    75→    required this.caption,
    76→    required this.code,
    77→    required this.overline,
    78→    required this.link,
    79→  });
    80→
    81→  /// Builds the standard Obers UI type scale.
    82→  ///
    83→  /// [fontFamily] is applied to every style except [code].
    84→  /// [monoFontFamily] is applied to [code].  When omitted both fall back to
    85→  /// system defaults.
    86→  factory OiTextTheme.standard({String? fontFamily, String? monoFontFamily}) {
    87→    const defaultMono = 'monospace';
    88→    return OiTextTheme(
    89→      display: TextStyle(
    90→        fontFamily: fontFamily,
    91→        fontSize: 56,
    92→        fontWeight: FontWeight.w700,
    93→        height: 1.1,
    94→        letterSpacing: -0.5,
    95→      ),
    96→      h1: TextStyle(
    97→        fontFamily: fontFamily,
    98→        fontSize: 40,
    99→        fontWeight: FontWeight.w700,
   100→        height: 1.2,
   101→        letterSpacing: -0.25,
   102→      ),
   103→      h2: TextStyle(
   104→        fontFamily: fontFamily,
   105→        fontSize: 32,
   106→        fontWeight: FontWeight.w600,
   107→        height: 1.25,
   108→      ),
   109→      h3: TextStyle(
   110→        fontFamily: fontFamily,
   111→        fontSize: 24,
   112→        fontWeight: FontWeight.w600,
   113→        height: 1.3,
   114→      ),
   115→      h4: TextStyle(
   116→        fontFamily: fontFamily,
   117→        fontSize: 20,
   118→        fontWeight: FontWeight.w600,
   119→        height: 1.35,
   120→      ),
   121→      body: TextStyle(
   122→        fontFamily: fontFamily,
   123→        fontSize: 16,
   124→        fontWeight: FontWeight.w400,
   125→        height: 1.5,
   126→      ),
   127→      bodyStrong: TextStyle(
   128→        fontFamily: fontFamily,
   129→        fontSize: 16,
   130→        fontWeight: FontWeight.w600,
   131→        height: 1.5,
   132→      ),
   133→      small: TextStyle(
   134→        fontFamily: fontFamily,
   135→        fontSize: 14,
   136→        fontWeight: FontWeight.w400,
   137→        height: 1.5,
   138→      ),
   139→      smallStrong: TextStyle(
   140→        fontFamily: fontFamily,
   141→        fontSize: 14,
   142→        fontWeight: FontWeight.w600,
   143→        height: 1.5,
   144→      ),
   145→      tiny: TextStyle(
   146→        fontFamily: fontFamily,
   147→        fontSize: 12,
   148→        fontWeight: FontWeight.w400,
   149→        height: 1.5,
   150→      ),
   151→      caption: TextStyle(
   152→        fontFamily: fontFamily,
   153→        fontSize: 12,
   154→        fontWeight: FontWeight.w400,
   155→        height: 1.4,
   156→      ),
   157→      code: TextStyle(
   158→        fontFamily: monoFontFamily ?? defaultMono,
   159→        fontSize: 14,
   160→        fontWeight: FontWeight.w400,
   161→        height: 1.6,
   162→      ),
   163→      overline: TextStyle(
   164→        fontFamily: fontFamily,
   165→        fontSize: 11,
   166→        fontWeight: FontWeight.w600,
   167→        height: 1.4,
   168→        letterSpacing: 1.5,
   169→      ),
   170→      link: TextStyle(
   171→        fontFamily: fontFamily,
   172→        fontSize: 16,
   173→        fontWeight: FontWeight.w400,
   174→        height: 1.5,
   175→        decoration: TextDecoration.underline,
   176→      ),
   177→    );
   178→  }
   179→
   180→  /// Linearly interpolates between two [OiTextTheme]s.
   181→  ///
   182→  /// [t] must be in the range [0, 1]. At [t] = 0 the result equals [a];
   183→  /// at [t] = 1 the result equals [b].
   184→  OiTextTheme.lerp(OiTextTheme a, OiTextTheme b, double t)
   185→    : display = TextStyle.lerp(a.display, b.display, t)!,
   186→      h1 = TextStyle.lerp(a.h1, b.h1, t)!,
   187→      h2 = TextStyle.lerp(a.h2, b.h2, t)!,
   188→      h3 = TextStyle.lerp(a.h3, b.h3, t)!,
   189→      h4 = TextStyle.lerp(a.h4, b.h4, t)!,
   190→      body = TextStyle.lerp(a.body, b.body, t)!,
   191→      bodyStrong = TextStyle.lerp(a.bodyStrong, b.bodyStrong, t)!,
   192→      small = TextStyle.lerp(a.small, b.small, t)!,
   193→      smallStrong = TextStyle.lerp(a.smallStrong, b.smallStrong, t)!,
   194→      tiny = TextStyle.lerp(a.tiny, b.tiny, t)!,
   195→      caption = TextStyle.lerp(a.caption, b.caption, t)!,
   196→      code = TextStyle.lerp(a.code, b.code, t)!,
   197→      overline = TextStyle.lerp(a.overline, b.overline, t)!,
   198→      link = TextStyle.lerp(a.link, b.link, t)!;
   199→
   200→  // ── Styles ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   201→
   202→  /// Hero / marketing display text — largest size.
   203→  final TextStyle display;
   204→
   205→  /// Top-level heading.
   206→  final TextStyle h1;
   207→
   208→  /// Second-level heading.
   209→  final TextStyle h2;
   210→
   211→  /// Third-level heading.
   212→  final TextStyle h3;
   213→
   214→  /// Fourth-level heading.
   215→  final TextStyle h4;
   216→
   217→  /// Standard paragraph body text.
   218→  final TextStyle body;
   219→
   220→  /// Bold / emphasized paragraph body text.
   221→  final TextStyle bodyStrong;
   222→
   223→  /// Small body text, suitable for secondary information.
   224→  final TextStyle small;
   225→
   226→  /// Bold small body text.
   227→  final TextStyle smallStrong;
   228→
   229→  /// Extra-small text, suitable for dense UI elements.
   230→  final TextStyle tiny;
   231→
   232→  /// Caption text below images or media.
   233→  final TextStyle caption;
   234→
   235→  /// Monospace code / preformatted text.
   236→  final TextStyle code;
   237→
   238→  /// All-caps label with letter-spacing, used above sections.
   239→  final TextStyle overline;
   240→
   241→  /// Inline hyperlink text.
   242→  final TextStyle link;
   243→
   244→  // ── Utilities ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   245→
   246→  /// Returns the [TextStyle] corresponding to the given [OiLabelVariant].
   247→  TextStyle styleFor(OiLabelVariant variant) {
   248→    switch (variant) {
   249→      case OiLabelVariant.display:
   250→        return display;
   251→      case OiLabelVariant.h1:
   252→        return h1;
   253→      case OiLabelVariant.h2:
   254→        return h2;
   255→      case OiLabelVariant.h3:
   256→        return h3;
   257→      case OiLabelVariant.h4:
   258→        return h4;
   259→      case OiLabelVariant.body:
   260→        return body;
   261→      case OiLabelVariant.bodyStrong:
   262→        return bodyStrong;
   263→      case OiLabelVariant.small:
   264→        return small;
   265→      case OiLabelVariant.smallStrong:
   266→        return smallStrong;
   267→      case OiLabelVariant.tiny:
   268→        return tiny;
   269→      case OiLabelVariant.caption:
   270→        return caption;
   271→      case OiLabelVariant.code:
   272→        return code;
   273→      case OiLabelVariant.overline:
   274→        return overline;
   275→      case OiLabelVariant.link:
   276→        return link;
   277→    }
   278→  }
   279→
   280→  /// Creates a copy of this theme with the specified styles replaced.
   281→  OiTextTheme copyWith({
   282→    TextStyle? display,
   283→    TextStyle? h1,
   284→    TextStyle? h2,
   285→    TextStyle? h3,
   286→    TextStyle? h4,
   287→    TextStyle? body,
   288→    TextStyle? bodyStrong,
   289→    TextStyle? small,
   290→    TextStyle? smallStrong,
   291→    TextStyle? tiny,
   292→    TextStyle? caption,
   293→    TextStyle? code,
   294→    TextStyle? overline,
   295→    TextStyle? link,
   296→  }) {
   297→    return OiTextTheme(
   298→      display: display ?? this.display,
   299→      h1: h1 ?? this.h1,
   300→      h2: h2 ?? this.h2,
   301→      h3: h3 ?? this.h3,
   302→      h4: h4 ?? this.h4,
   303→      body: body ?? this.body,
   304→      bodyStrong: bodyStrong ?? this.bodyStrong,
   305→      small: small ?? this.small,
   306→      smallStrong: smallStrong ?? this.smallStrong,
   307→      tiny: tiny ?? this.tiny,
   308→      caption: caption ?? this.caption,
   309→      code: code ?? this.code,
   310→      overline: overline ?? this.overline,

   317→    if (identical(this, other)) return true;

   320→        other.h1 == h1 &&

   323→        other.h4 == h4 &&
   324→        other.body == body &&

     3→/// The spacing scale for the design system.
     4→///
     5→/// Provides named spacing values used for padding, margins, and gaps
     6→/// throughout the library. All values are in logical pixels.
     7→///
     8→/// {@category Foundation}
     9→@immutable
    10→class OiSpacingScale {
    11→  /// Creates an [OiSpacingScale] with explicit values.
    12→  const OiSpacingScale({
    13→    required this.xs,
    14→    required this.sm,
    15→    required this.md,
    16→    required this.lg,
    17→    required this.xl,
    18→    required this.xxl,
    19→    required this.pageGutterCompact,
    20→    required this.pageGutterMedium,
    21→    required this.pageGutterExpanded,
    22→    required this.pageGutterLarge,
    23→    required this.pageGutterExtraLarge,
    24→  });
    25→
    26→  /// Creates the standard spacing scale.
    27→  ///
    28→  /// Values follow a 4dp base grid with doubling progression.
    29→  factory OiSpacingScale.standard() {
    30→    return const OiSpacingScale(
    31→      xs: 4,
    32→      sm: 8,
    33→      md: 16,
    34→      lg: 24,
    35→      xl: 32,
    36→      xxl: 48,
    37→      pageGutterCompact: 16,
    38→      pageGutterMedium: 24,
    39→      pageGutterExpanded: 32,
    40→      pageGutterLarge: 40,
    41→      pageGutterExtraLarge: 48,
    42→    );
    43→  }
    44→
    45→  /// Extra-small spacing (4dp). Used for tight component-internal gaps.
    46→  final double xs;
    47→
    48→  /// Small spacing (8dp). Used for element gaps and compact padding.
    49→  final double sm;
    50→
    51→  /// Medium spacing (16dp). Default padding and standard gaps.
    52→  final double md;
    53→
    54→  /// Large spacing (24dp). Section padding and generous gaps.
    55→  final double lg;
    56→
    57→  /// Extra-large spacing (32dp). Card padding and section separators.
    58→  final double xl;
    59→
    60→  /// Double extra-large spacing (48dp). Major section separators.
    61→  final double xxl;
    62→
    63→  /// Page gutter at the compact breakpoint (<600dp).
    64→  final double pageGutterCompact;
    65→
    66→  /// Page gutter at the medium breakpoint (600–840dp).
    67→  final double pageGutterMedium;
    68→
    69→  /// Page gutter at the expanded breakpoint (840–1200dp).
    70→  final double pageGutterExpanded;
    71→
    72→  /// Page gutter at the large breakpoint (1200–1600dp).
    73→  final double pageGutterLarge;
    74→
    75→  /// Page gutter at the extra-large breakpoint (>1600dp).
    76→  final double pageGutterExtraLarge;

     3→/// Configuration for animation durations and motion preferences.
     4→///
     5→/// When [reducedMotion] is true (e.g. when the user has enabled the system
     6→/// "Reduce Motion" accessibility setting), all animated durations resolve to
     7→/// [Duration.zero] to eliminate motion for users who are sensitive to it.
     8→///
     9→/// {@category Foundation}
    10→@immutable
    11→class OiAnimationConfig {

    13→  const OiAnimationConfig({

    15→    required this.normal,
    16→    required this.slow,
    17→    required this.reducedMotion,
    18→  });
    19→
    20→  /// Creates the standard animation configuration with typical durations.
    21→  ///
    22→  /// Set [reducedMotion] to true to eliminate all animations (e.g. when
    23→  /// `MediaQuery.disableAnimations` is true).
    24→  const OiAnimationConfig.standard({this.reducedMotion = false})
    25→    : fast = reducedMotion ? Duration.zero : const Duration(milliseconds: 150),
    26→      normal = reducedMotion
    27→          ? Duration.zero
    28→          : const Duration(milliseconds: 250),
    29→      slow = reducedMotion ? Duration.zero : const Duration(milliseconds: 400);
    30→
    31→  /// Fast transition duration (150ms). Used for micro-interactions.
    32→  final Duration fast;
    33→
    34→  /// Normal transition duration (250ms). Default for most animations.
    35→  final Duration normal;
    36→
    37→  /// Slow transition duration (400ms). Used for large layout changes.
    38→  final Duration slow;
    39→
    40→  /// When true, all durations resolve to [Duration.zero].
    41→  ///
    42→  /// Should mirror [MediaQueryData.disableAnimations].
    43→  final bool reducedMotion;
    44→
    45→  /// Creates a copy with optionally overridden values.
    46→  OiAnimationConfig copyWith({
    47→    Duration? fast,
    48→    Duration? normal,
    49→    Duration? slow,
    50→    bool? reducedMotion,
    51→  }) {
    52→    return OiAnimationConfig(
    53→      fast: fast ?? this.fast,
    54→      normal: normal ?? this.normal,
    55→      slow: slow ?? this.slow,
    56→      reducedMotion: reducedMotion ?? this.reducedMotion,
    57→    );
    58→  }
    59→
    60→  @override
    61→  bool operator ==(Object other) {
    62→    if (identical(this, other)) return true;
    63→    return other is OiAnimationConfig &&
    64→        other.fast == fast &&
    65→        other.normal == normal &&
    66→        other.slow == slow &&
    67→        other.reducedMotion == reducedMotion;
    68→  }
    69→
    70→  @override
    71→  int get hashCode => Object.hash(fast, normal, slow, reducedMotion);
    72→}

    74→/// Configuration for device performance tiers.
    75→///
    76→/// Lower-performance devices can opt out of expensive visual effects
    77→/// such as blur, shadows, and complex animations. Use [OiPerformanceConfig.auto]
    78→/// to detect the tier automatically based on device capabilities.
    79→///
    80→/// {@category Foundation}
    81→@immutable
    82→class OiPerformanceConfig {
    83→  /// Creates a [OiPerformanceConfig] with explicit settings.
    84→  const OiPerformanceConfig({
    85→    required this.disableBlur,
    86→    required this.disableShadows,
    87→    required this.reduceAnimations,
    88→    required this.disableHalo,
    89→    required this.animationScale,
    90→  });
    91→
    92→  /// High-performance configuration. All effects enabled.
    93→  const OiPerformanceConfig.high()
    94→    : disableBlur = false,
    95→      disableShadows = false,
    96→      reduceAnimations = false,
    97→      disableHalo = false,
    98→      animationScale = 1.0;
    99→
   100→  /// Mid-performance configuration. Blur disabled, other effects enabled.
   101→  const OiPerformanceConfig.mid()
   102→    : disableBlur = true,
   103→      disableShadows = false,
   104→      reduceAnimations = false,
   105→      disableHalo = false,
   106→      animationScale = 1.0;
   107→
   108→  /// Low-performance configuration. All expensive effects disabled.
   109→  const OiPerformanceConfig.low()
   110→    : disableBlur = true,
   111→      disableShadows = true,
   112→      reduceAnimations = true,
   113→      disableHalo = true,
   114→      animationScale = 0.5;
   115→
   116→  /// Automatically selects a tier based on the current platform.
   117→  ///
   118→  /// On web: mid (blur is expensive in browsers).
   119→  /// Otherwise: high.
   120→  factory OiPerformanceConfig.auto() {
   121→    if (kIsWeb) return const OiPerformanceConfig.mid();
   122→    return const OiPerformanceConfig.high();
   123→  }
   124→
   125→  /// When true, [BackdropFilter] blur effects are not rendered.
   126→  final bool disableBlur;
   127→
   128→  /// When true, [BoxShadow] effects are not applied.
   129→  final bool disableShadows;
   130→
   131→  /// When true, animation durations are halved via [animationScale].
   132→  final bool reduceAnimations;
   133→
   134→  /// When true, halo/glow effects around interactive elements are suppressed.
   135→  final bool disableHalo;
   136→
   137→  /// Multiplier applied to all animation durations. Default is 1.0.
   138→  final double animationScale;
   139→
   140→  /// Creates a copy with optionally overridden values.
   141→  OiPerformanceConfig copyWith({
   142→    bool? disableBlur,
   143→    bool? disableShadows,
   144→    bool? reduceAnimations,
   145→    bool? disableHalo,
   146→    double? animationScale,
   147→  }) {
   148→    return OiPerformanceConfig(
   149→      disableBlur: disableBlur ?? this.disableBlur,
   150→      disableShadows: disableShadows ?? this.disableShadows,
   151→      reduceAnimations: reduceAnimations ?? this.reduceAnimations,
   152→      disableHalo: disableHalo ?? this.disableHalo,
   153→      animationScale: animationScale ?? this.animationScale,
   154→    );
   155→  }
   156→
   157→  @override
   158→  bool operator ==(Object other) {
   159→    if (identical(this, other)) return true;
   160→    return other is OiPerformanceConfig &&
   161→        other.disableBlur == disableBlur &&
   162→        other.disableShadows == disableShadows &&
   163→        other.reduceAnimations == reduceAnimations &&
   164→        other.disableHalo == disableHalo &&
   165→        other.animationScale == animationScale;
   166→  }
   167→
   168→  @override
   169→  int get hashCode => Object.hash(
   170→    disableBlur,
   171→    disableShadows,
   172→    reduceAnimations,
   173→    disableHalo,
   174→    animationScale,
   175→  );
   176→}

   160→/// Describes a gradient used for backgrounds, borders, or overlays.
   161→///
   162→/// Supports both linear and radial gradients. Call [toGradient] to obtain
   163→/// the corresponding Flutter [Gradient] object for rendering.
   164→///
   165→/// {@category Foundation}
   166→@immutable
   167→class OiGradientStyle {
   168→  /// Creates an [OiGradientStyle] with all fields specified explicitly.
   169→  const OiGradientStyle({
   170→    required this.linear,
   171→    required this.colors,
   172→    this.stops,
   173→    this.begin,
   174→    this.end,
   175→    this.center,
   176→    this.radius,
   177→  });
   178→
   179→  /// Creates a linear gradient from [colors].
   180→  ///
   181→  /// [stops] optionally specifies the position of each color (0.0–1.0).
   182→  /// [begin] and [end] define the direction; they default to
   183→  /// [AlignmentDirectional.centerStart] and [AlignmentDirectional.centerEnd].
   184→  factory OiGradientStyle.linear(
   185→    List<Color> colors, {
   186→    List<double>? stops,
   187→    AlignmentGeometry? begin,
   188→    AlignmentGeometry? end,
   189→  }) {
   190→    return OiGradientStyle(
   191→      linear: true,
   192→      colors: colors,
   193→      stops: stops,
   194→      begin: begin,
   195→      end: end,
   196→    );
   197→  }
   198→
   199→  /// Creates a radial gradient from [colors].
   200→  ///
   201→  /// [stops] optionally specifies the position of each color (0.0–1.0).
   202→  /// [center] and [radius] define the focal point and size.
   203→  factory OiGradientStyle.radial(
   204→    List<Color> colors, {
   205→    List<double>? stops,
   206→    AlignmentGeometry? center,
   207→    double? radius,
   208→  }) {
   209→    return OiGradientStyle(
   210→      linear: false,
   211→      colors: colors,
   212→      stops: stops,
   213→      center: center,
   214→      radius: radius,
   215→    );
   216→  }
   217→
   218→  /// Whether this gradient is linear (`true`) or radial (`false`).
   219→  final bool linear;
   220→
   221→  /// The list of colors in the gradient.
   222→  final List<Color> colors;
   223→
   224→  /// Optional color stops. Must have the same length as [colors] when provided.
   225→  final List<double>? stops;
   226→
   227→  /// The start alignment for a linear gradient.
   228→  final AlignmentGeometry? begin;
   229→
   230→  /// The end alignment for a linear gradient.
   231→  final AlignmentGeometry? end;
   232→
   233→  /// The center alignment for a radial gradient.
   234→  final AlignmentGeometry? center;
   235→
   236→  /// The radius for a radial gradient.
   237→  final double? radius;
   238→
   239→  /// Converts this style to a Flutter [Gradient] for rendering.
   240→  ///
   241→  /// Returns a [LinearGradient] when [linear] is `true`, otherwise a
   242→  /// [RadialGradient].
   243→  Gradient toGradient() {
   244→    if (linear) {
   245→      return LinearGradient(
   246→        colors: colors,
   247→        stops: stops,
   248→        begin: begin ?? Alignment.centerLeft,
   249→        end: end ?? Alignment.centerRight,
   250→      );
   251→    }
   252→    return RadialGradient(
   253→      colors: colors,
   254→      stops: stops,
   255→      center: center ?? Alignment.center,
   256→      radius: radius ?? 0.5,
   257→    );
   258→  }
   259→
   260→  /// Creates a copy with optionally overridden values.
   261→  OiGradientStyle copyWith({
   262→    bool? linear,
   263→    List<Color>? colors,
   264→    List<double>? stops,
   265→    AlignmentGeometry? begin,
   266→    AlignmentGeometry? end,
   267→    AlignmentGeometry? center,
   268→    double? radius,
   269→  }) {
   270→    return OiGradientStyle(
   271→      linear: linear ?? this.linear,
   272→      colors: colors ?? this.colors,
   273→      stops: stops ?? this.stops,
   274→      begin: begin ?? this.begin,
   275→      end: end ?? this.end,
   276→      center: center ?? this.center,
   277→      radius: radius ?? this.radius,
   278→    );
   279→  }
   280→
   281→  @override
   282→  bool operator ==(Object other) {
   283→    if (identical(this, other)) return true;
   284→    if (other is! OiGradientStyle) return false;
   285→    if (colors.length != other.colors.length) return false;
   286→    for (var i = 0; i < colors.length; i++) {
   287→      if (colors[i] != other.colors[i]) return false;
   288→    }
   289→    if (stops != null && other.stops != null) {
   290→      if (stops!.length != other.stops!.length) return false;
   291→      for (var i = 0; i < stops!.length; i++) {
   292→        if (stops![i] != other.stops![i]) return false;
   293→      }
   294→    } else if (stops != other.stops) {
   295→      return false;
   296→    }
   297→    return other.linear == linear &&
   298→        other.begin == begin &&
   299→        other.end == end &&
   300→        other.center == center &&
   301→        other.radius == radius;
   302→  }
   303→
   304→  @override
   305→  int get hashCode => Object.hash(
   306→    linear,
   307→    Object.hashAll(colors),
   308→    Object.hashAll(stops ?? const []),
   309→    begin,
   310→    end,
   311→    center,
   312→    radius,
   313→  );
   314→}

   316→/// The complete decoration theme for the design system.
   317→///
   318→/// Holds the standard border styles for default, focused, and error states,
   319→/// as well as a named gradient palette.
   320→///

   330→  });
   331→
   332→  /// Creates the standard decoration theme from [primaryColor] and [errorColor].
   333→  factory OiDecorationTheme.standard({

   339→        const Color(0xFFD1D5DB),
   340→        1,
   341→        borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(8),
   342→      ),
   343→      focusBorder: OiBorderStyle.solid(
   344→        primaryColor,
   345→        2,
   346→        borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(8),
   347→      ),
   348→      errorBorder: OiBorderStyle.solid(
   349→        errorColor,
   350→        2,
   351→        borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(8),
   352→      ),
   353→      gradients: {
   354→        'primary': OiGradientStyle.linear([
   355→          primaryColor,
   356→          primaryColor.withValues(alpha: 0.7),
   357→        ]),
   358→        'error': OiGradientStyle.linear([
   359→          errorColor,

   361→        ]),
   362→      },

   365→
   366→  /// The default border style used on unfocused, non-error inputs and containers.
   367→  final OiBorderStyle defaultBorder;
   368→
   369→  /// The border style applied when an element has keyboard focus.
   370→  final OiBorderStyle focusBorder;
   371→
   372→  /// The border style applied when an element has a validation error.
   373→  final OiBorderStyle errorBorder;
   374→
   375→  /// A named map of gradient styles available to components.
   376→  final Map<String, OiGradientStyle> gradients;
   377→
   378→  /// Creates a copy with optionally overridden values.
   379→  OiDecorationTheme copyWith({
   380→    OiBorderStyle? defaultBorder,

   384→  }) {

   387→      focusBorder: focusBorder ?? this.focusBorder,
   388→      errorBorder: errorBorder ?? this.errorBorder,
   389→      gradients: gradients ?? this.gradients,
   390→    );
   391→  }
   392→
   393→  @override
   394→  bool operator ==(Object other) {
   395→    if (identical(this, other)) return true;
   396→    if (other is! OiDecorationTheme) return false;
   397→    if (other.defaultBorder != defaultBorder) return false;
   398→    if (other.focusBorder != focusBorder) return false;
   399→    if (other.errorBorder != errorBorder) return false;
   400→    if (gradients.length != other.gradients.length) return false;
   401→    for (final key in gradients.keys) {
   402→      if (gradients[key] != other.gradients[key]) return false;

   410→    focusBorder,
   411→    errorBorder,

   413→  );

     4→/// Defines a halo/glow effect rendered as a [BoxShadow].

     6→/// Halo effects are used to highlight interactive elements (e.g. focused
     7→/// inputs, hovered buttons) with a subtle colored glow.

    10→@immutable

    16→    required this.blur,

    20→  ///
    21→  /// The [intensity] factor (0.0–1.0) scales the spread and opacity.
    22→  factory OiHaloStyle.from(Color color, {double intensity = 0.3}) {
    23→    return OiHaloStyle(
    24→      color: color.withValues(alpha: intensity),
    25→      spread: 2,
    26→      blur: 8,
    27→    );
    28→  }

    30→  /// No halo effect.

    34→    blur: 0,
    35→  );

    39→
    40→  /// The shadow spread radius in logical pixels.

    43→  /// The shadow blur radius in logical pixels.

    46→  /// Converts this halo style to a [BoxShadow] for rendering.

    48→    return BoxShadow(color: color, spreadRadius: spread, blurRadius: blur);
    49→  }
    50→
    51→  /// Creates a copy with optionally overridden values.
    52→  OiHaloStyle copyWith({Color? color, double? spread, double? blur}) {
    53→    return OiHaloStyle(
    54→      color: color ?? this.color,
    55→      spread: spread ?? this.spread,
    56→      blur: blur ?? this.blur,
    57→    );
    58→  }

   118→/// The complete set of interactive state effects for the design system.

     2→import 'package:flutter/painting.dart';
     3→
     4→/// A color token with five semantic shade variants.
     5→///
     6→/// Each swatch represents a single brand/semantic color with automatically
     7→/// derived or explicitly provided shade variants. Used throughout
     8→/// [OiColorScheme] to represent primary, accent, success, warning, error,
     9→/// and info colors.
    10→///
    11→/// {@category Foundation}
    12→@immutable
    13→class OiColorSwatch {
    14→  /// Creates an [OiColorSwatch] with explicit shade values.
    15→  const OiColorSwatch({
    16→    required this.base,
    17→    required this.light,
    18→    required this.dark,
    19→    required this.muted,
    20→    required this.foreground,
    21→  });
    22→
    23→  /// Derives shade variants from [base] using HSL color manipulation.
    24→  ///
    25→  /// - [light]: increased lightness (+20%)
    26→  /// - [dark]: decreased lightness (-20%)
    27→  /// - [muted]: desaturated to 40%, increased lightness (+30%)
    28→  /// - [foreground]: white or black depending on contrast with [base]
    29→  factory OiColorSwatch.from(Color base) {
    30→    final hsl = HSLColor.fromColor(base);
    31→    final light = hsl
    32→        .withLightness((hsl.lightness + 0.20).clamp(0.0, 1.0))
    33→        .toColor();
    34→    final dark = hsl
    35→        .withLightness((hsl.lightness - 0.20).clamp(0.0, 1.0))
    36→        .toColor();
    37→    final muted = hsl
    38→        .withSaturation((hsl.saturation * 0.4).clamp(0.0, 1.0))
    39→        .withLightness((hsl.lightness + 0.30).clamp(0.0, 1.0))
    40→        .toColor();
    41→    // WCAG contrast: use white on dark backgrounds, black on light.
    42→    final luminance = base.computeLuminance();
    43→    final foreground = luminance < 0.35
    44→        ? const Color(0xFFFFFFFF)
    45→        : const Color(0xFF000000);
    46→    return OiColorSwatch(
    47→      base: base,
    48→      light: light,
    49→      dark: dark,
    50→      muted: muted,
    51→      foreground: foreground,
    52→    );
    53→  }
    54→
    55→  /// Linearly interpolates between two [OiColorSwatch]es.
    56→  ///
    57→  /// [t] must be in the range [0, 1]. At [t] = 0 the result equals [a];
    58→  /// at [t] = 1 the result equals [b].
    59→  OiColorSwatch.lerp(OiColorSwatch a, OiColorSwatch b, double t)
    60→    : base = Color.lerp(a.base, b.base, t)!,
    61→      light = Color.lerp(a.light, b.light, t)!,
    62→      dark = Color.lerp(a.dark, b.dark, t)!,
    63→      muted = Color.lerp(a.muted, b.muted, t)!,
    64→      foreground = Color.lerp(a.foreground, b.foreground, t)!;
    65→
    66→  /// The primary/base shade.
    67→  final Color base;
    68→
    69→  /// A lighter variant, suitable for backgrounds and hover states.
    70→  final Color light;
    71→
    72→  /// A darker variant, suitable for pressed/active states.
    73→  final Color dark;
    74→
    75→  /// A muted/desaturated variant, suitable for disabled or subtle contexts.
    76→  final Color muted;
    77→
    78→  /// A foreground color (white or black) with sufficient contrast over [base].
    79→  final Color foreground;
    80→
    81→  /// Creates a copy with optionally overridden fields.
    82→  OiColorSwatch copyWith({
    83→    Color? base,
    84→    Color? light,
    85→    Color? dark,
    86→    Color? muted,
    87→    Color? foreground,
    88→  }) {
    89→    return OiColorSwatch(
    90→      base: base ?? this.base,
    91→      light: light ?? this.light,
    92→      dark: dark ?? this.dark,
    93→      muted: muted ?? this.muted,
    94→      foreground: foreground ?? this.foreground,
    95→    );
    96→  }
    97→
    98→  @override
    99→  bool operator ==(Object other) {
   100→    if (identical(this, other)) return true;
   101→    return other is OiColorSwatch &&
   102→        other.base == base &&
   103→        other.light == light &&
   104→        other.dark == dark &&
   105→        other.muted == muted &&
   106→        other.foreground == foreground;
   107→  }
   108→
   109→  @override
   110→  int get hashCode => Object.hash(base, light, dark, muted, foreground);
   111→
   112→  @override
   113→  String toString() =>
   114→      'OiColorSwatch('
   115→      'base: $base, '
   116→      'light: $light, '
   117→      'dark: $dark, '
   118→      'muted: $muted, '
   119→      'foreground: $foreground)';
   120→}

     6→/// Utility for generating dynamic themes from brand parameters.
     7→///
     8→/// This is a convenience wrapper around [OiThemeData.fromBrand] that
     9→/// provides additional utilities for runtime theme generation.
    10→class OiDynamicTheme {
    11→  // Private constructor to prevent instantiation.
    12→  OiDynamicTheme._();
    13→
    14→  /// Generates a complete [OiThemeData] from a single brand color.
    15→  ///
    16→  /// Delegates to [OiThemeData.fromBrand] with the given parameters.
    17→  static OiThemeData fromColor(
    18→    Color color, {
    19→    Brightness brightness = Brightness.light,
    20→    String? fontFamily,
    21→    OiRadiusPreference radiusPreference = OiRadiusPreference.medium,
    22→  }) => OiThemeData.fromBrand(
    23→    color: color,
    24→    brightness: brightness,
    25→    fontFamily: fontFamily,
    26→    radiusPreference: radiusPreference,
    27→  );
    28→
    29→  /// Generates light and dark variants from a single brand color.
    30→  ///
    31→  /// Returns a record containing both the light and dark [OiThemeData]
    32→  /// derived from the given [color].
    33→  static ({OiThemeData light, OiThemeData dark}) fromColorPair(
    34→    Color color, {
    35→    String? fontFamily,
    36→    OiRadiusPreference radiusPreference = OiRadiusPreference.medium,
    37→  }) {
    38→    return (
    39→      light: OiThemeData.fromBrand(
    40→        color: color,
    41→        brightness: Brightness.light,
    42→        fontFamily: fontFamily,
    43→        radiusPreference: radiusPreference,
    44→      ),
    45→      dark: OiThemeData.fromBrand(
    46→        color: color,
    47→        brightness: Brightness.dark,
    48→        fontFamily: fontFamily,
    49→        radiusPreference: radiusPreference,
    50→      ),
    51→    );
    52→  }
    53→
    54→  /// Generates a theme from a seed color by extracting the dominant color.
    55→  ///
    56→  /// Returns the [OiThemeData.fromBrand] result using the provided
    57→  /// [seedColor] as the brand color.
    58→  static OiThemeData fromSeedColor(
    59→    Color seedColor, {
    60→    Brightness brightness = Brightness.light,
    61→  }) => OiThemeData.fromBrand(color: seedColor, brightness: brightness);
    62→}
    63→

    10→/// A storybook-like interface to browse every component.
    11→///
    12→/// Renders a catalog of all obers_ui components organized by category,
    13→/// with prop toggles and theme switching. Intended for use in the
    14→/// example app or as a development tool.
    15→///
    16→/// {@category Tools}
    17→class OiPlayground extends StatefulWidget {
    18→  /// Creates an [OiPlayground].
    19→  const OiPlayground({
    20→    super.key,
    21→    this.theme,
    22→    this.darkTheme,
    23→    this.initialCategory,
    24→  });
    25→
    26→  /// The light theme to use. Defaults to [OiThemeData.light].
    27→  final OiThemeData? theme;
    28→
    29→  /// The dark theme. Defaults to [OiThemeData.dark].
    30→  final OiThemeData? darkTheme;
    31→
    32→  /// The initial category to display.
    33→  final String? initialCategory;
    34→
    35→  @override
    36→  State<OiPlayground> createState() => _OiPlaygroundState();
    37→}
    38→
    39→class _OiPlaygroundState extends State<OiPlayground> {
    40→  late String _selectedCategory;
    41→  bool _isDark = false;
    42→
    43→  /// The categories available in the playground.
    44→  static const categories = [
    45→    'Buttons',
    46→    'Inputs',
    47→    'Display',
    48→    'Feedback',
    49→    'Overlays',
    50→    'Navigation',
    51→  ];
    52→
    53→  @override
    54→  void initState() {
    55→    super.initState();
    56→    _selectedCategory = widget.initialCategory ?? categories.first;
    57→  }
    58→
    59→  OiThemeData get _effectiveTheme {
    60→    if (_isDark) {
    61→      return widget.darkTheme ?? OiThemeData.dark();
    62→    }
    63→    return widget.theme ?? OiThemeData.light();
    64→  }
    65→
    66→  void _toggleTheme() {
    67→    setState(() => _isDark = !_isDark);
    68→  }
    69→
    70→  void _selectCategory(String category) {
    71→    setState(() => _selectedCategory = category);
    72→  }
    73→
    74→  @override
    75→  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    76→    return OiTheme(
    77→      data: _effectiveTheme,
    78→      child: Builder(
    79→        builder: (context) {
    80→          final colors = context.colors;
    81→          return ColoredBox(
    82→            color: colors.background,
    83→            child: Column(
    84→              crossAxisAlignment: CrossAxisAlignment.stretch,
    85→              children: [
    86→                // Header
    87→                _buildHeader(context),
    88→                // Body
    89→                Expanded(
    90→                  child: Row(
    91→                    children: [
    92→                      // Sidebar
    93→                      _buildSidebar(context),
    94→                      // Content area
    95→                      Expanded(child: _buildContent(context)),
    96→                    ],
    97→                  ),
    98→                ),
    99→              ],
   100→            ),
   101→          );
   102→        },
   103→      ),
   104→    );
   105→  }
   106→
   107→  Widget _buildHeader(BuildContext context) {
   108→    final colors = context.colors;
   109→    return Container(
   110→      padding: const EdgeInsets.symmetric(horizontal: 16, vertical: 8),
   111→      color: colors.surface,
   112→      child: Row(
   113→        children: [
   114→          Text(
   115→            'OiPlayground',
   116→            key: const ValueKey('playground_title'),
   117→            style: TextStyle(
   118→              fontWeight: FontWeight.bold,
   119→              fontSize: 16,
   120→              color: colors.text,
   121→            ),
   122→          ),
   123→          const Spacer(),
   124→          GestureDetector(
   125→            key: const ValueKey('playground_theme_toggle'),
   126→            onTap: _toggleTheme,
   127→            child: Container(
   128→              padding: const EdgeInsets.symmetric(horizontal: 12, vertical: 4),
   129→              decoration: BoxDecoration(
   130→                color: colors.surfaceSubtle,
   131→                borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(4),
   132→              ),
   133→              child: Text(
   134→                _isDark ? 'Light' : 'Dark',
   135→                style: TextStyle(fontSize: 12, color: colors.text),
   136→              ),
   137→            ),
   138→          ),
   139→        ],
   140→      ),
   141→    );
   142→  }
   143→
   144→  Widget _buildSidebar(BuildContext context) {
   145→    final colors = context.colors;
   146→    return Container(
   147→      width: 160,
   148→      color: colors.surfaceSubtle,
   149→      child: ListView(
   150→        children: [
   151→          for (final category in categories)
   152→            GestureDetector(
   153→              key: ValueKey('playground_cat_$category'),
   154→              onTap: () => _selectCategory(category),
   155→              child: Container(
   156→                padding: const EdgeInsets.symmetric(
   157→                  horizontal: 12,
   158→                  vertical: 8,
   159→                ),
   160→                color: _selectedCategory == category
   161→                    ? colors.primary.base.withValues(alpha: 0.1)
   162→                    : null,
   163→                child: Text(
   164→                  category,
   165→                  style: TextStyle(
   166→                    color: _selectedCategory == category
   167→                        ? colors.primary.base
   168→                        : colors.text,
   169→                    fontWeight: _selectedCategory == category
   170→                        ? FontWeight.bold
   171→                        : FontWeight.normal,
   172→                    fontSize: 13,
   173→                  ),
   174→                ),
   175→              ),
   176→            ),
   177→        ],
   178→      ),
   179→    );
   180→  }
   181→
   182→  Widget _buildContent(BuildContext context) {
   183→    final colors = context.colors;
   184→    return Padding(
   185→      padding: const EdgeInsets.all(16),
   186→      child: Column(
   187→        crossAxisAlignment: CrossAxisAlignment.start,
   188→        children: [
   189→          Text(
   190→            _selectedCategory,
   191→            key: const ValueKey('playground_category_title'),
   192→            style: TextStyle(
   193→              fontSize: 18,
   194→              fontWeight: FontWeight.bold,
   195→              color: colors.text,
   196→            ),
   197→          ),
   198→          const SizedBox(height: 16),
   199→          Expanded(child: _buildSamples(context)),
   200→        ],
   201→      ),
   202→    );
   203→  }
   204→
   205→  Widget _buildSamples(BuildContext context) {
   206→    switch (_selectedCategory) {
   207→      case 'Buttons':
   208→        return _buildButtonSamples(context);
   209→      case 'Inputs':
   210→        return _buildInputSamples(context);
   211→      case 'Display':
   212→        return _buildDisplaySamples(context);
   213→      default:
   214→        return Center(
   215→          child: Text(
   216→            'Coming soon: $_selectedCategory',
   217→            style: TextStyle(color: context.colors.textMuted),
   218→          ),
   219→        );
   220→    }
   221→  }
   222→
   223→  Widget _buildButtonSamples(BuildContext context) {
   224→    return Wrap(
   225→      spacing: 8,
   226→      runSpacing: 8,
   227→      children: [
   228→        OiButton.primary(label: 'Primary', onTap: () {}),
   229→        OiButton.secondary(label: 'Secondary', onTap: () {}),
   230→        OiButton.outline(label: 'Outline', onTap: () {}),
   231→        OiButton.ghost(label: 'Ghost', onTap: () {}),
   232→        const OiButton.primary(label: 'Disabled'),
   233→      ],
   234→    );
   235→  }
   236→
   237→  Widget _buildInputSamples(BuildContext context) {
   238→    return ListView(
   239→      children: const [
   240→        OiTextInput(label: 'Text input'),
   241→        SizedBox(height: 12),
   242→        OiTextInput(label: 'Disabled input', enabled: false),
   243→      ],
   244→    );
   245→  }
   246→
   247→  Widget _buildDisplaySamples(BuildContext context) {
   248→    return ListView(
   249→      children: [
   250→        const OiCard(child: Text('Sample card')),
   251→        const SizedBox(height: 12),
   252→        const OiBadge(label: 'Badge'),
   253→        const SizedBox(height: 12),
   254→        const OiProgress(value: 0.6, label: 'Progress'),
   255→      ],
   256→    );
   257→  }
   258→}
