1→import 'package:flutter/widgets.dart';
     2→import 'package:obers_ui/src/components/buttons/oi_button.dart';
     3→
     4→/// A button that displays only an icon.
     5→///
     6→/// This is a thin wrapper around [OiButton.icon] that exposes the same
     7→/// parameters as a regular [StatelessWidget] for convenience.
     8→///
     9→/// ```dart
    10→/// OiIconButton(
    11→///   icon: Icons.add,
    12→///   onTap: () => doSomething(),
    13→/// )
    14→/// ```
    15→///
    16→/// {@category Components}
    17→class OiIconButton extends StatelessWidget {
    18→  /// Creates an [OiIconButton].
    19→  const OiIconButton({
    20→    required this.icon,
    21→    required this.semanticLabel,
    22→    this.onTap,
    23→    this.size = OiButtonSize.medium,
    24→    this.variant = OiButtonVariant.ghost,
    25→    this.enabled = true,
    26→    super.key,
    27→  });
    28→
    29→  /// The icon to display inside the button.
    30→  final IconData icon;
    31→
    32→  /// Called when the button is tapped.
    33→  final VoidCallback? onTap;
    34→
    35→  /// The size tier.
    36→  final OiButtonSize size;
    37→
    38→  /// The visual style variant.
    39→  final OiButtonVariant variant;
    40→
    41→  /// Whether the button responds to interactions.
    42→  final bool enabled;
    43→
    44→  /// Accessibility label announced by screen readers.
    45→  final String semanticLabel;
    46→
    47→  @override
    48→  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    49→    return OiButton.icon(
    50→      icon: icon,
    51→      onTap: onTap,
    52→      size: size,
    53→      variant: variant,
    54→      enabled: enabled,
    55→      label: semanticLabel,
    56→    );
    57→  }
    58→}
    59→

1→import 'package:flutter/widgets.dart';
     2→import 'package:obers_ui/src/foundation/theme/oi_text_theme.dart';
     3→import 'package:obers_ui/src/foundation/theme/oi_theme.dart';
     4→
     5→/// A semantically-annotated icon widget.
     6→///
     7→/// [OiIcon] wraps an [IconData] with an [Icon] widget and adds a
     8→/// [Semantics] node so that screen readers can announce the icon's purpose.
     9→///
    10→/// Use [OiIcon.decorative] for purely decorative icons that should be
    11→/// excluded from the accessibility tree.
    12→///
    13→/// The [size] defaults to the body font size from the active theme when not
    14→/// supplied.
    15→///
    16→/// {@category Primitives}
    17→class OiIcon extends StatelessWidget {
    18→  /// Creates a semantic [OiIcon].
    19→  ///
    20→  /// [label] is announced by screen readers; it is required so that
    21→  /// every meaningful icon has an accessible description.
    22→  const OiIcon({
    23→    required this.icon,
    24→    required this.label,
    25→    this.size,
    26→    this.color,
    27→    super.key,
    28→  }) : _decorative = false;
    29→
    30→  /// Creates a purely decorative [OiIcon] that is excluded from the
    31→  /// accessibility tree.
    32→  const OiIcon.decorative({
    33→    required this.icon,
    34→    this.size,
    35→    this.color,
    36→    super.key,
    37→  }) : label = '',
    38→       _decorative = true;
    39→
    40→  /// The icon glyph to render.
    41→  final IconData icon;
    42→
    43→  /// The accessibility label announced by screen readers.
    44→  ///
    45→  /// Empty for decorative icons created via [OiIcon.decorative].
    46→  final String label;
    47→
    48→  /// The size of the icon in logical pixels.
    49→  ///
    50→  /// Defaults to the body font size from the nearest [OiTheme].
    51→  final double? size;
    52→
    53→  /// The color of the icon.
    54→  ///
    55→  /// When null the nearest [DefaultTextStyle] color is used.
    56→  final Color? color;
    57→
    58→  final bool _decorative;
    59→
    60→  @override
    61→  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    62→    final resolvedSize =
    63→        size ??
    64→        context.textTheme.styleFor(OiLabelVariant.body).fontSize ??
    65→        16.0;
    66→
    67→    final iconWidget = Icon(icon, size: resolvedSize, color: color);
    68→
    69→    if (_decorative) {
    70→      return ExcludeSemantics(child: iconWidget);
    71→    }
    72→
    73→    return Semantics(
    74→      label: label,
    75→      image: true,
    76→      child: ExcludeSemantics(child: iconWidget),
    77→    );
    78→  }
    79→}

1→import 'package:flutter/widgets.dart';
     2→
     3→/// A flexible image widget with semantics, loading states, and error handling.
     4→///
     5→/// [OiImage] renders a network or asset image and wraps it in a
     6→/// [Semantics] node so that screen readers can describe the image.
     7→///
     8→/// - For URLs beginning with `http://` or `https://`, [Image.network] is used.
     9→/// - For all other [src] values, [Image.asset] is used.
    10→/// - Supply [placeholder] to show a widget while the image loads.
    11→/// - Supply [errorWidget] to show a widget when loading fails.
    12→/// - Set [lazy] to `true` to opt into deferred loading behaviour (stored as
    13→///   metadata; full viewport-based lazy loading requires additional
    14→///   infrastructure that ships in a later phase).
    15→///
    16→/// Use [OiImage.decorative] for purely decorative images that should be
    17→/// excluded from the accessibility tree.
    18→///
    19→/// {@category Primitives}
    20→class OiImage extends StatelessWidget {
    21→  /// Creates an [OiImage] with an accessibility [alt] label.
    22→  const OiImage({
    23→    required this.src,
    24→    required this.alt,
    25→    this.width,
    26→    this.height,
    27→    this.fit,
    28→    this.placeholder,
    29→    this.errorWidget,
    30→    this.lazy = false,
    31→    super.key,
    32→  }) : _decorative = false;
    33→
    34→  /// Creates a purely decorative [OiImage] excluded from the accessibility
    35→  /// tree.
    36→  const OiImage.decorative({
    37→    required this.src,
    38→    this.width,
    39→    this.height,
    40→    this.fit,
    41→    this.placeholder,
    42→    this.errorWidget,
    43→    this.lazy = false,
    44→    super.key,
    45→  }) : alt = '',
    46→       _decorative = true;
    47→
    48→  /// The image source: a network URL (`http://` / `https://`) or asset path.
    49→  final String src;
    50→
    51→  /// The accessibility description announced by screen readers.
    52→  ///
    53→  /// Empty for decorative images created via [OiImage.decorative].
    54→  final String alt;
    55→
    56→  /// Optional width constraint in logical pixels.
    57→  final double? width;
    58→
    59→  /// Optional height constraint in logical pixels.
    60→  final double? height;
    61→
    62→  /// How the image should be inscribed into the allocated space.
    63→  final BoxFit? fit;
    64→
    65→  /// Widget shown while the image is loading (network images only).
    66→  final Widget? placeholder;
    67→
    68→  /// Widget shown when the image fails to load.
    69→  final Widget? errorWidget;
    70→
    71→  /// When `true`, deferred loading is requested.
    72→  ///
    73→  /// This flag is stored for future use; full viewport-based lazy loading
    74→  /// will be implemented in a later phase.
    75→  final bool lazy;
    76→
    77→  final bool _decorative;
    78→
    79→  bool get _isNetwork =>
    80→      src.startsWith('http://') || src.startsWith('https://');
    81→
    82→  Widget _buildImage() {
    83→    if (_isNetwork) {
    84→      return Image.network(
    85→        src,
    86→        width: width,
    87→        height: height,
    88→        fit: fit,
    89→        filterQuality: lazy ? FilterQuality.low : FilterQuality.medium,
    90→        loadingBuilder: placeholder == null
    91→            ? null
    92→            : (context, child, loadingProgress) {
    93→                if (loadingProgress == null) return child;
    94→                return placeholder!;
    95→              },
    96→        errorBuilder: errorWidget == null
    97→            ? null
    98→            : (context, error, stackTrace) => errorWidget!,
    99→      );
   100→    }
   101→
   102→    return Image.asset(
   103→      src,
   104→      width: width,
   105→      height: height,
   106→      fit: fit,
   107→      filterQuality: lazy ? FilterQuality.low : FilterQuality.medium,
   108→      errorBuilder: errorWidget == null
   109→          ? null
   110→          : (context, error, stackTrace) => errorWidget!,
   111→    );
   112→  }
   113→
   114→  @override
   115→  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
   116→    final image = _buildImage();
   117→
   118→    if (_decorative) {
   119→      return ExcludeSemantics(child: image);
   120→    }
   121→
   122→    return Semantics(image: true, label: alt, child: image);
   123→  }

## QA Structural Review Report

### Foundation Layer

**1. lib/src/foundation/oi_platform.dart**
- Classes: `OiPlatformData` (immutable snapshot), `OiPlatform` (InheritedWidget)
- Key properties: platform, keyboardHeight, keyboardVisible
- Features: Static factory `fromContext()`, `.of()` and `.maybeOf()` accessors, extension on BuildContext

**2. lib/src/foundation/oi_responsive.dart**
- Classes: `OiBreakpoint` (breakpoint definition), `OiResponsiveValue<T>` (resolution helper), `OiHeightBreakpoint` (enum)
- Key properties: name, minWidth; compact/medium/expanded/large/extraLarge static constants
- Extension methods: breakpoint, isCompact, isMedium, isExpanded, isLarge, isExtraLarge, adaptive, responsive, pageGutter, contentMaxWidth

**3. lib/src/foundation/oi_overlays.dart**
- Classes: `OiOverlayZOrder` (enum), `OiOverlayHandle`, `OiOverlaysService`, `OiOverlays` (InheritedWidget)
- Key methods: show(), dismissAll(), dismiss(), update() on handle
- Supports z-order layers: base, dropdown, tooltip, panel, dialog, toast, critical

**4. lib/src/foundation/oi_undo_stack.dart**
- Classes: `OiUndoAction` (immutable action), `OiUndoStack` (ChangeNotifier), `OiUndoStackProvider` (InheritedNotifier)
- Key methods: push(), undo(), redo(), clear(); properties: canUndo, canRedo, history, future, nextUndoLabel, nextRedoLabel
- Features: Action merging with groupId, merge callback support, max history limit

**5. lib/src/foundation/persistence/oi_settings_driver.dart**
- Classes: `OiSettingsDriver` (abstract base)
- Key abstract methods: load<T>, save<T>, delete(), exists()
- Helper: resolveKey() for namespace::key format

**6. lib/src/foundation/persistence/oi_settings_provider.dart**
- Classes: `OiSettingsProvider` (InheritedWidget)
- Static accessors: of(), maybeOf()

---

### Components - Buttons

**7. lib/src/components/buttons/oi_button.dart**
- Classes: `OiButton` (StatefulWidget), internal state `_OiButtonState`
- Enums: `OiButtonSize` (small/medium/large), `OiButtonVariant` (primary/secondary/outline/ghost/destructive/soft), `OiIconPosition` (leading/trailing)
- Special constructors: `.primary()`, `.secondary()`, `.outline()`, `.ghost()`, `.destructive()`, `.soft()`, `.icon()`, `.split()`, `.countdown()`, `.confirm()`
- Key properties: label, icon, variant, size, enabled, loading, fullWidth, semanticLabel, dropdown, countdownSeconds, confirmLabel
- State tracking: confirm pending, dropdown visibility, countdown timer

**8. lib/src/components/buttons/oi_button_group.dart**
- Classes: `OiButtonGroup` (StatelessWidget), internal `_GroupButtonItem`
- Key properties: buttons list, orientation (Axis), segmented mode, selectedIndex, onSelected callback
- Features: Connected visual appearance with shared borders

**9. lib/src/components/buttons/oi_icon_button.dart**
- Classes: `OiIconButton` (StatelessWidget wrapper)
- Wraps `OiButton.icon()` with convenience parameters

**10. lib/src/components/buttons/oi_toggle_button.dart**
- Classes: `OiToggleButton` (StatelessWidget)
- Key properties: selected, label, icon, size, enabled, semanticLabel, onChanged callback
- Features: Toggles between filled primary and ghost/outline styles

---

### Components - Internal

**11. lib/src/components/_internal/oi_input_frame.dart**
- Classes: `OiInputFrame` (StatelessWidget, internal)
- Key properties: child, label, hint, error, focused, enabled, readOnly, leading, trailing, padding
- Features: Border resolution (error/focus/default), label/hint/error display, opacity when disabled

---

### Primitives - Display

**12. lib/src/primitives/display/oi_surface.dart**
- Classes: `OiSurface` (StatelessWidget), internal painter `_OiBorderPainter`
- Key properties: color, border, borderRadius, shadow, padding, halo, glass, gradient, child
- Features: Solid/gradient/dashed/dotted borders, drop shadows, halo glow, frosted-glass backdrop blur

**13. lib/src/primitives/display/oi_icon.dart**
- Classes: `OiIcon` (StatelessWidget)
- Constructors: standard (semantic), `.decorative()`
- Key properties: icon, label, size, color
- Features: Accessibility semantics, decorative exclusion

**14. lib/src/primitives/display/oi_image.dart**
- Classes: `OiImage` (StatelessWidget)
- Constructors: standard (with alt), `.decorative()`
- Key properties: src (network/asset URL detection), alt, width, height, fit, placeholder, errorWidget, lazy flag
- Features: Network vs asset routing, lazy loading metadata

**15. lib/src/primitives/display/oi_divider.dart**
- Classes: `OiDivider` (StatelessWidget), internal painter `_OiDividerPainter`
- Constructors: plain, `.withLabel()`, `.withContent()`
- Key properties: axis, thickness, color, style (solid/dashed/dotted), spacing
- Features: Centered content, dashed/dotted custom painting

---

### Primitives - Interaction

**16. lib/src/primitives/interaction/oi_tappable.dart**
- Classes: `OiTappable` (StatefulWidget), internal `_OiTappableState`, render objects `_TouchTargetPadding`, `_RenderTouchTargetPadding`
- Key properties: child, onTap, onDoubleTap, onLongPress, onHover, onFocusChange, enabled, focusable, semanticLabel, cursor
- Features: Press/hover/focus state tracking, animated feedback, WCAG 48dp touch target enforcement, disabled opacity (0.4)

---

### Primitives - Overlay

**17. lib/src/primitives/overlay/oi_floating.dart**
- Classes: `OiFloating` (StatefulWidget), internal `_OiFloatingState`
- Enum: `OiFloatingAlignment` (12 positions: topStart/topCenter/topEnd, bottomStart/bottomCenter/bottomEnd, leftStart/leftCenter/leftEnd, rightStart/rightCenter/rightEnd)
- Key properties: anchor, child, visible, alignment, gap, autoFlip, bottomSheetOnCompact, offset
- Features: CompositedTransformFollower positioning, bottom-sheet fallback on compact, auto-flip on viewport overflow

**18. lib/src/primitives/overlay/oi_portal.dart**
- Classes: `OiPortal` (StatefulWidget), internal `_OiPortalState`
- Key properties: child, active boolean
- Features: Deferred overlay insertion/removal via SchedulerBinding, maintains state/animation during hide

**19. lib/src/primitives/overlay/oi_visibility.dart**
- Classes: `OiVisibility` (StatefulWidget), internal `_OiVisibilityState` with SingleTickerProviderStateMixin
- Enum: `OiTransition` (none/fade/fadeScale/slideUp/slideDown/slideLeft/slideRight)
- Constructors: standard, `.responsive()` for breakpoint-specific transitions
- Key properties: visible, child, transition, maintainState
- Features: AnimationController-based transitions, reduced-motion support via A11y check

---

### Primitives - Layout

**20. lib/src/primitives/layout/oi_grid.dart**
- Classes: `OiGrid` (StatelessWidget)
- Key properties: children, columns (fixed) or minColumnWidth (auto-compute), gap, rowGap
- Features: LayoutBuilder-based responsive column count, Wrap-based layout

**21. lib/src/primitives/layout/oi_spacer.dart**
- Classes: `OiSpacer` (StatelessWidget)
- Key properties: size (fixed), flex (flexible), axis
- Features: Dual-mode spacer (flexible Spacer or fixed SizedBox)

**22. lib/src/primitives/layout/oi_aspect_ratio.dart**
- Classes: `OiAspectRatio` (StatelessWidget)
- Key properties: ratio, child
- Features: Wraps Flutter AspectRatio with named parameter clarity

---

## Summary

All files are well-structured following Flutter conventions. Key QA observations:
- Consistent use of immutable classes and InheritedWidget patterns for state sharing
- Theme integration via context extensions (colors, spacing, radius, effects, animations)
- Accessibility support across visual and interactive components
- Responsive design integration (breakpoints, density awareness)
- State management through StatefulWidget and ChangeNotifier patterns
- Custom painting for advanced border effects (dashes, gradients)
- Proper WCAG compliance (48dp touch targets, semantic labels, reduced motion)

1→import 'package:flutter/widgets.dart';
     2→import 'package:obers_ui/src/foundation/theme/oi_theme.dart';
     3→
     4→/// A time slot in the scheduler.
     5→///
     6→/// Represents a time-bounded appointment displayed in an [OiScheduler].
     7→@immutable
     8→class OiScheduleSlot {
     9→  /// Creates an [OiScheduleSlot].
    10→  const OiScheduleSlot({
    11→    required this.key,
    12→    required this.title,
    13→    required this.start,
    14→    required this.end,
    15→    this.color,
    16→    this.data,
    17→  });
    18→
    19→  /// A unique identifier for this slot.
    20→  final Object key;
    21→
    22→  /// The display title for the slot.
    23→  final String title;
    24→
    25→  /// The start date/time of the slot.
    26→  final DateTime start;
    27→
    28→  /// The end date/time of the slot.
    29→  final DateTime end;
    30→
    31→  /// An optional color for the slot chip.
    32→  final Color? color;
    33→
    34→  /// Optional arbitrary data associated with this slot.
    35→  final Map<String, dynamic>? data;
    36→}
    37→
    38→/// Scheduler display mode.
    39→///
    40→/// Determines whether the scheduler shows a single day or a full week.
    41→enum OiSchedulerMode {
    42→  /// A single day view.
    43→  day,
    44→
    45→  /// A full week view.
    46→  week,
    47→}
    48→
    49→/// A day/week schedule view for managing time-based appointments.
    50→///
    51→/// Supports navigation between days/weeks, slot display, and interaction
    52→/// callbacks for tapping slots, tapping empty time cells, and moving slots.
    53→///
    54→/// {@category Composites}
    55→class OiScheduler extends StatefulWidget {
    56→  /// Creates an [OiScheduler].
    57→  const OiScheduler({
    58→    required this.slots,
    59→    required this.label,
    60→    super.key,
    61→    this.date,
    62→    this.mode = OiSchedulerMode.day,
    63→    this.startHour = 8,
    64→    this.endHour = 18,
    65→    this.onSlotTap,
    66→    this.onTimeSlotTap,
    67→    this.onSlotMove,
    68→  });
    69→
    70→  /// The list of schedule slots to display.
    71→  final List<OiScheduleSlot> slots;
    72→
    73→  /// An accessibility label describing this scheduler.
    74→  final String label;
    75→
    76→  /// The date the scheduler focuses on. Defaults to today.
    77→  final DateTime? date;
    78→
    79→  /// The display mode: day or week.
    80→  final OiSchedulerMode mode;
    81→
    82→  /// The first hour displayed in the schedule grid.
    83→  final int startHour;
    84→
    85→  /// The last hour displayed in the schedule grid (exclusive).
    86→  final int endHour;
    87→
    88→  /// Called when an existing slot is tapped.
    89→  final ValueChanged<OiScheduleSlot>? onSlotTap;
    90→
    91→  /// Called when an empty time cell is tapped.
    92→  final ValueChanged<DateTime>? onTimeSlotTap;
    93→
    94→  /// Called when a slot is moved to a new time range.
    95→  final void Function(OiScheduleSlot slot, DateTime start, DateTime end)?
    96→  onSlotMove;
    97→
    98→  @override
    99→  State<OiScheduler> createState() => _OiSchedulerState();
   100→}
   101→
   102→class _OiSchedulerState extends State<OiScheduler> {
   103→  late DateTime _focusDate;
   104→  late OiSchedulerMode _mode;
   105→
   106→  @override
   107→  void initState() {
   108→    super.initState();
   109→    _focusDate = widget.date ?? DateTime.now();
   110→    _mode = widget.mode;
   111→  }
   112→
   113→  // ── Navigation ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   114→
   115→  void _goForward() {
   116→    setState(() {
   117→      switch (_mode) {
   118→        case OiSchedulerMode.day:
   119→          _focusDate = _focusDate.add(const Duration(days: 1));
   120→        case OiSchedulerMode.week:
   121→          _focusDate = _focusDate.add(const Duration(days: 7));
   122→      }
   123→    });
   124→  }
   125→
   126→  void _goBackward() {
   127→    setState(() {
   128→      switch (_mode) {
   129→        case OiSchedulerMode.day:
   130→          _focusDate = _focusDate.subtract(const Duration(days: 1));
   131→        case OiSchedulerMode.week:
   132→          _focusDate = _focusDate.subtract(const Duration(days: 7));
   133→      }
   134→    });
   135→  }
   136→
   137→  void _setMode(OiSchedulerMode mode) {
   138→    setState(() => _mode = mode);
   139→  }
   140→
   141→  // ── Helpers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   142→
   143→  /// Whether [a] and [b] fall on the same calendar day.
   144→  bool _sameDay(DateTime a, DateTime b) =>
   145→      a.year == b.year && a.month == b.month && a.day == b.day;
   146→
   147→  /// Returns the start of the week (Monday) containing [_focusDate].
   148→  DateTime _weekStart() {
   149→    final offset = (_focusDate.weekday - DateTime.monday + 7) % 7;
   150→    return DateTime(_focusDate.year, _focusDate.month, _focusDate.day - offset);
   151→  }
   152→
   153→  /// The ordered day-of-week headers starting from Monday.
   154→  static const _dayHeaders = ['Mon', 'Tue', 'Wed', 'Thu', 'Fri', 'Sat', 'Sun'];
   155→
   156→  /// The number of hour rows to display.
   157→  int get _hourCount => widget.endHour - widget.startHour;
   158→
   159→  /// The title for the navigation header.
   160→  String get _headerTitle {
   161→    const months = [
   162→      'January',
   163→      'February',
   164→      'March',
   165→      'April',
   166→      'May',
   167→      'June',
   168→      'July',
   169→      'August',
   170→      'September',
   171→      'October',
   172→      'November',
   173→      'December',
   174→    ];
   175→    switch (_mode) {
   176→      case OiSchedulerMode.day:
   177→        final m = months[_focusDate.month - 1];
   178→        return '${_focusDate.day} $m ${_focusDate.year}';
   179→      case OiSchedulerMode.week:
   180→        final ws = _weekStart();
   181→        final we = ws.add(const Duration(days: 6));
   182→        return '${ws.day}/${ws.month} - ${we.day}/${we.month} ${we.year}';
   183→    }
   184→  }
   185→
   186→  /// Returns slots that overlap the given [date] and [hour].
   187→  List<OiScheduleSlot> _slotsAt(DateTime date, int hour) {
   188→    return widget.slots.where((s) {
   189→      if (!_sameDay(s.start, date) && !_sameDay(s.end, date)) return false;
   190→      return s.start.hour <= hour && s.end.hour > hour;
   191→    }).toList();
   192→  }
   193→
   194→  // ── Build ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   195→
   196→  @override
   197→  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
   198→    return Semantics(
   199→      label: widget.label,
   200→      child: Column(
   201→        crossAxisAlignment: CrossAxisAlignment.stretch,
   202→        children: [
   203→          _buildHeader(context),
   204→          _buildModeSelector(context),
   205→          Expanded(child: _buildBody(context)),
   206→        ],
   207→      ),
   208→    );
   209→  }
   210→
   211→  Widget _buildHeader(BuildContext context) {
   212→    final colors = context.colors;
   213→    return Padding(
   214→      padding: const EdgeInsets.symmetric(horizontal: 8, vertical: 4),
   215→      child: Row(
   216→        children: [
   217→          GestureDetector(
   218→            key: const ValueKey('scheduler_back'),
   219→            onTap: _goBackward,
   220→            child: Padding(
   221→              padding: const EdgeInsets.all(8),
   222→              child: Text('\u25C0', style: TextStyle(color: colors.text)),
   223→            ),
   224→          ),
   225→          Expanded(
   226→            child: Center(
   227→              child: Text(
   228→                _headerTitle,
   229→                key: const ValueKey('scheduler_header_title'),
   230→                style: TextStyle(
   231→                  fontWeight: FontWeight.bold,
   232→                  color: colors.text,
   233→                ),
   234→              ),
   235→            ),
   236→          ),
   237→          GestureDetector(
   238→            key: const ValueKey('scheduler_forward'),
   239→            onTap: _goForward,
   240→            child: Padding(
   241→              padding: const EdgeInsets.all(8),
   242→              child: Text('\u25B6', style: TextStyle(color: colors.text)),
   243→            ),
   244→          ),
   245→        ],
   246→      ),
   247→    );
   248→  }
   249→
   250→  Widget _buildModeSelector(BuildContext context) {
   251→    final colors = context.colors;
   252→    return Padding(
   253→      padding: const EdgeInsets.only(bottom: 4),
   254→      child: Row(
   255→        mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.center,
   256→        children: [
   257→          for (final mode in OiSchedulerMode.values)
   258→            Padding(
   259→              padding: const EdgeInsets.symmetric(horizontal: 4),
   260→              child: GestureDetector(
   261→                key: ValueKey('scheduler_mode_${mode.name}'),
   262→                onTap: () => _setMode(mode),
   263→                child: Container(
   264→                  padding: const EdgeInsets.symmetric(
   265→                    horizontal: 12,
   266→                    vertical: 4,
   267→                  ),
   268→                  decoration: BoxDecoration(
   269→                    color: _mode == mode ? colors.primary.base : colors.surface,
   270→                    borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(4),
   271→                  ),
   272→                  child: Text(
   273→                    mode.name[0].toUpperCase() + mode.name.substring(1),
   274→                    style: TextStyle(
   275→                      color: _mode == mode ? colors.textOnPrimary : colors.text,
   276→                      fontSize: 12,
   277→                    ),
   278→                  ),
   279→                ),
   280→              ),
   281→            ),
   282→        ],
   283→      ),
   284→    );
   285→  }
   286→
   287→  Widget _buildBody(BuildContext context) {
   288→    switch (_mode) {
   289→      case OiSchedulerMode.day:
   290→        return _buildDayView(context);
   291→      case OiSchedulerMode.week:
   292→        return _buildWeekView(context);
   293→    }
   294→  }
   295→
   296→  // ── Day view ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   297→
   298→  Widget _buildDayView(BuildContext context) {
   299→    final colors = context.colors;
   300→
   301→    return ListView.builder(
   302→      itemCount: _hourCount,
   303→      itemBuilder: (context, index) {
   304→        final hour = widget.startHour + index;
   305→        final cellTime = DateTime(
   306→          _focusDate.year,
   307→          _focusDate.month,
   308→          _focusDate.day,
   309→          hour,
   310→        );
   311→        final hourSlots = _slotsAt(_focusDate, hour);
   312→
   313→        return GestureDetector(
   314→          behavior: HitTestBehavior.opaque,
   315→          onTap: widget.onTimeSlotTap != null
   316→              ? () => widget.onTimeSlotTap!(cellTime)
   317→              : null,
   318→          child: Container(
   319→            height: 48,
   320→            decoration: BoxDecoration(
   321→              border: Border(bottom: BorderSide(color: colors.borderSubtle)),
   322→            ),
   323→            child: Row(
   324→              children: [
   325→                SizedBox(
   326→                  width: 48,
   327→                  child: Center(
   328→                    child: Text(
   329→                      '${hour.toString().padLeft(2, '0')}:00',
   330→                      style: TextStyle(fontSize: 10, color: colors.textMuted),
   331→                    ),
   332→                  ),
   333→                ),
   334→                Expanded(
   335→                  child: hourSlots.isEmpty
   336→                      ? const SizedBox.shrink()
   337→                      : Column(
   338→                          crossAxisAlignment: CrossAxisAlignment.stretch,
   339→                          children: [
   340→                            for (final slot in hourSlots)
   341→                              Expanded(
   342→                                child: GestureDetector(
   343→                                  onTap: widget.onSlotTap != null
   344→                                      ? () => widget.onSlotTap!(slot)
   345→                                      : null,
   346→                                  child: Container(
   347→                                    padding: const EdgeInsets.all(4),
   348→                                    margin: const EdgeInsets.all(1),
   349→                                    decoration: BoxDecoration(
   350→                                      color: slot.color ?? colors.primary.base,
   351→                                      borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(3),
   352→                                    ),
   353→                                    child: Text(
   354→                                      slot.title,
   355→                                      maxLines: 1,
   356→                                      overflow: TextOverflow.ellipsis,
   357→                                      style: TextStyle(
   358→                                        fontSize: 11,
   359→                                        color: colors.textOnPrimary,
   360→                                      ),
   361→                                    ),
   362→                                  ),
   363→                                ),
   364→                              ),
   365→                          ],
   366→                        ),
   367→                ),
   368→              ],
   369→            ),
   370→          ),
   371→        );
   372→      },
   373→    );
   374→  }
   375→
   376→  // ── Week view ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   377→
   378→  Widget _buildWeekView(BuildContext context) {
   379→    final colors = context.colors;
   380→    final ws = _weekStart();
   381→
   382→    return Column(
   383→      children: [
   384→        // Day headers.
   385→        Row(
   386→          children: [
   387→            const SizedBox(width: 48),
   388→            for (var i = 0; i < 7; i++)
   389→              Expanded(
   390→                child: Center(
   391→                  child: Builder(
   392→                    builder: (_) {
   393→                      final d = ws.add(Duration(days: i));
   394→                      return Text(
   395→                        '${_dayHeaders[i]} ${d.day}',
   396→                        style: TextStyle(
   397→                          fontSize: 11,
   398→                          fontWeight: FontWeight.normal,
   399→                          color: colors.textMuted,
   400→                        ),
   401→                      );
   402→                    },
   403→                  ),
   404→                ),
   405→              ),
   406→          ],
   407→        ),
   408→        const SizedBox(height: 4),
   409→        // Hour rows.
   410→        Expanded(
   411→          child: ListView.builder(
   412→            itemCount: _hourCount,
   413→            itemBuilder: (context, index) {
   414→              final hour = widget.startHour + index;
   415→              return SizedBox(
   416→                height: 48,
   417→                child: Row(
   418→                  children: [
   419→                    SizedBox(
   420→                      width: 48,
   421→                      child: Center(
   422→                        child: Text(
   423→                          '${hour.toString().padLeft(2, '0')}:00',
   424→                          style: TextStyle(
   425→                            fontSize: 10,
   426→                            color: colors.textMuted,
   427→                          ),
   428→                        ),
   429→                      ),
   430→                    ),
   431→                    for (var dayIdx = 0; dayIdx < 7; dayIdx++)
   432→                      Expanded(
   433→                        child: _buildWeekCell(
   434→                          context,
   435→                          ws.add(Duration(days: dayIdx)),
   436→                          hour,
   437→                        ),
   438→                      ),
   439→                  ],
   440→                ),
   441→              );
   442→            },
   443→          ),
   444→        ),
   445→      ],
   446→    );
   447→  }
   448→
   449→  Widget _buildWeekCell(BuildContext context, DateTime date, int hour) {
   450→    final colors = context.colors;
   451→    final cellTime = DateTime(date.year, date.month, date.day, hour);
   452→    final cellSlots = _slotsAt(date, hour);
   453→
   454→    return GestureDetector(
   455→      behavior: HitTestBehavior.opaque,
   456→      onTap: widget.onTimeSlotTap != null
   457→          ? () => widget.onTimeSlotTap!(cellTime)
   458→          : null,
   459→      child: Container(
   460→        margin: const EdgeInsets.all(0.5),
   461→        decoration: BoxDecoration(
   462→          border: Border.all(color: colors.borderSubtle.withValues(alpha: 0.3)),
   463→        ),
   464→        child: cellSlots.isEmpty
   465→            ? null
   466→            : Column(
   467→                crossAxisAlignment: CrossAxisAlignment.stretch,
   468→                children: [
   469→                  for (final slot in cellSlots.take(1))
   470→                    Expanded(
   471→                      child: GestureDetector(
   472→                        onTap: widget.onSlotTap != null
   473→                            ? () => widget.onSlotTap!(slot)
   474→                            : null,
   475→                        child: Container(
   476→                          padding: const EdgeInsets.all(2),
   477→                          color: slot.color ?? colors.primary.base,
   478→                          child: Text(
   479→                            slot.title,
   480→                            maxLines: 1,
   481→                            overflow: TextOverflow.ellipsis,
   482→                            style: TextStyle(
   483→                              fontSize: 9,
   484→                              color: colors.textOnPrimary,
   485→                            ),
   486→                          ),
   487→                        ),
   488→                      ),
   489→                    ),
   490→                ],
   491→              ),
   492→      ),
   493→    );
   494→  }
   495→}

1→import 'package:flutter/widgets.dart';
     2→import 'package:obers_ui/src/foundation/theme/oi_theme.dart';
     3→
     4→/// A stage in the funnel.
     5→///
     6→/// Each stage has a [label], a [value] representing its magnitude,
     7→/// and an optional [color] override.
     8→class OiFunnelStage {
     9→  /// Creates an [OiFunnelStage].
    10→  const OiFunnelStage({required this.label, required this.value, this.color});
    11→
    12→  /// The human-readable label for this stage.
    13→  final String label;
    14→
    15→  /// The numeric value of this stage.
    16→  final double value;
    17→
    18→  /// An optional override color. When null, a color from the theme's
    19→  /// chart palette is used.
    20→  final Color? color;
    21→}
    22→
    23→/// A funnel chart showing conversion through stages.
    24→///
    25→/// Stages are rendered as progressively narrowing trapezoids from top to
    26→/// bottom. Each stage can optionally display its [value] and the percentage
    27→/// relative to the first stage.
    28→///
    29→/// An [onStageTap] callback is invoked when a stage is tapped.
    30→///
    31→/// {@category Composites}
    32→class OiFunnelChart extends StatelessWidget {
    33→  /// Creates an [OiFunnelChart].
    34→  const OiFunnelChart({
    35→    required this.stages,
    36→    required this.label,
    37→    super.key,
    38→    this.showValues = true,
    39→    this.showPercentages = true,
    40→    this.formatValue,
    41→    this.onStageTap,
    42→  });
    43→
    44→  /// The funnel stages from widest (top) to narrowest (bottom).
    45→  final List<OiFunnelStage> stages;
    46→
    47→  /// The accessibility label for the chart.
    48→  final String label;
    49→
    50→  /// Whether to show the numeric value on each stage.
    51→  final bool showValues;
    52→
    53→  /// Whether to show the percentage relative to the first stage.
    54→  final bool showPercentages;
    55→
    56→  /// An optional callback to format stage values.
    57→  final String Function(double)? formatValue;
    58→
    59→  /// Called when a stage is tapped, with the stage index.
    60→  final ValueChanged<int>? onStageTap;
    61→
    62→  @override
    63→  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    64→    final colors = context.colors;
    65→    final chartColors = colors.chart;
    66→
    67→    if (stages.isEmpty) {
    68→      return Semantics(
    69→        label: label,
    70→        child: const SizedBox.shrink(key: Key('oi_funnel_chart_empty')),
    71→      );
    72→    }
    73→
    74→    final maxValue = stages.first.value;
    75→
    76→    return Semantics(
    77→      label: label,
    78→      child: LayoutBuilder(
    79→        builder: (context, constraints) {
    80→          final availableWidth = constraints.maxWidth;
    81→          const stageHeight = 48.0;
    82→
    83→          return Column(
    84→            key: const Key('oi_funnel_chart'),
    85→            mainAxisSize: MainAxisSize.min,
    86→            children: [
    87→              for (var i = 0; i < stages.length; i++)
    88→                _buildStage(
    89→                  context: context,
    90→                  index: i,
    91→                  stage: stages[i],
    92→                  maxValue: maxValue,
    93→                  availableWidth: availableWidth,
    94→                  stageHeight: stageHeight,
    95→                  color: stages[i].color ?? chartColors[i % chartColors.length],
    96→                  textColor: colors.textOnPrimary,
    97→                  percentColor: colors.textMuted,
    98→                ),
    99→            ],
   100→          );
   101→        },
   102→      ),
   103→    );
   104→  }
   105→
   106→  Widget _buildStage({
   107→    required BuildContext context,
   108→    required int index,
   109→    required OiFunnelStage stage,
   110→    required double maxValue,
   111→    required double availableWidth,
   112→    required double stageHeight,
   113→    required Color color,
   114→    required Color textColor,
   115→    required Color percentColor,
   116→  }) {
   117→    final ratio = maxValue > 0 ? (stage.value / maxValue).clamp(0.0, 1.0) : 0.0;
   118→    // Minimum width of 20% so the narrowest stage is still visible.
   119→    final stageWidth = availableWidth * (0.2 + 0.8 * ratio);
   120→
   121→    final formattedValue =
   122→        formatValue?.call(stage.value) ?? stage.value.toStringAsFixed(0);
   123→    final percentage = maxValue > 0
   124→        ? (stage.value / maxValue * 100).toStringAsFixed(0)
   125→        : '0';
   126→
   127→    return GestureDetector(
   128→      onTap: onStageTap != null ? () => onStageTap!(index) : null,
   129→      child: Padding(
   130→        padding: const EdgeInsets.symmetric(vertical: 2),
   131→        child: CustomPaint(
   132→          key: Key('oi_funnel_stage_$index'),
   133→          painter: _OiFunnelStagePainter(color: color),
   134→          child: SizedBox(
   135→            width: stageWidth,
   136→            height: stageHeight,
   137→            child: Center(
   138→              child: Padding(
   139→                padding: const EdgeInsets.symmetric(horizontal: 8),
   140→                child: Column(
   141→                  mainAxisSize: MainAxisSize.min,
   142→                  children: [
   143→                    Text(
   144→                      stage.label,
   145→                      style: TextStyle(
   146→                        color: textColor,
   147→                        fontSize: 12,
   148→                        fontWeight: FontWeight.w600,
   149→                      ),
   150→                      overflow: TextOverflow.ellipsis,
   151→                    ),
   152→                    if (showValues || showPercentages)
   153→                      Text(
   154→                        [
   155→                          if (showValues) formattedValue,
   156→                          if (showPercentages) '$percentage%',
   157→                        ].join(' - '),
   158→                        style: TextStyle(
   159→                          color: textColor.withValues(alpha: 0.8),
   160→                          fontSize: 10,
   161→                        ),
   162→                      ),
   163→                  ],
   164→                ),
   165→              ),
   166→            ),
   167→          ),
   168→        ),
   169→      ),
   170→    );
   171→  }
   172→}
   173→
   174→/// Paints a single funnel stage as a filled rounded rectangle.
   175→class _OiFunnelStagePainter extends CustomPainter {
   176→  const _OiFunnelStagePainter({required this.color});
   177→
   178→  /// The fill color for this stage.
   179→  final Color color;
   180→
   181→  @override
   182→  void paint(Canvas canvas, Size size) {
   183→    final paint = Paint()
   184→      ..color = color
   185→      ..style = PaintingStyle.fill;
   186→
   187→    final rrect = RRect.fromRectAndRadius(
   188→      Offset.zero & size,
   189→      const Radius.circular(6),
   190→    );
   191→    canvas.drawRRect(rrect, paint);
   192→  }
   193→
   194→  @override
   195→  bool shouldRepaint(_OiFunnelStagePainter oldDelegate) =>
   196→      oldDelegate.color != color;
   197→}

     7→/// A node in the flow graph.
     8→///
     9→/// Each node has a unique [key], a display [label], a [position] on the
    10→/// canvas, and lists of named [inputs] and [outputs] ports that edges can
    11→/// connect to.
    12→class OiFlowNode {
    13→  /// Creates an [OiFlowNode].
    14→  const OiFlowNode({
    15→    required this.key,
    16→    required this.position,
    17→    required this.label,
    18→    this.icon,
    19→    this.color,
    20→    this.inputs = const [],
    21→    this.outputs = const [],
    22→    this.data,
    23→  });
    24→
    25→  /// A unique key used to identify this node.
    26→  final Object key;
    27→
    28→  /// The position of the node on the graph canvas.
    29→  final Offset position;
    30→
    31→  /// The display label for this node.
    32→  final String label;
    33→
    34→  /// An optional icon displayed alongside the label.
    35→  final IconData? icon;
    36→
    37→  /// An optional color override for the node background.
    38→  final Color? color;
    39→
    40→  /// Named input ports on this node.
    41→  final List<String> inputs;
    42→
    43→  /// Named output ports on this node.
    44→  final List<String> outputs;
    45→
    46→  /// Optional arbitrary data associated with this node.
    47→  final Map<String, dynamic>? data;
    48→}
    49→
    50→/// An edge connecting two nodes in the flow graph.
    51→///
    52→/// Connects from [sourcePort] on [sourceNode] to [targetPort] on
    53→/// [targetNode].
    54→class OiFlowEdge {
    55→  /// Creates an [OiFlowEdge].
    56→  const OiFlowEdge({
    57→    required this.sourceNode,
    58→    required this.sourcePort,
    59→    required this.targetNode,
    60→    required this.targetPort,
    61→    this.label,
    62→    this.animated = false,
    63→  });
    64→
    65→  /// The key of the source [OiFlowNode].
    66→  final Object sourceNode;
    67→
    68→  /// The name of the output port on the source node.
    69→  final String sourcePort;
    70→
    71→  /// The key of the target [OiFlowNode].
    72→  final Object targetNode;
    73→
    74→  /// The name of the input port on the target node.
    75→  final String targetPort;
    76→
    77→  /// An optional label displayed alongside the edge.
    78→  final String? label;
    79→
    80→  /// Whether to animate the edge (e.g. dashed flow animation).
    81→  final bool animated;
    82→}
    83→
    84→/// A node-and-edge graph editor for visual workflows.
    85→///
    86→/// Supports rendering nodes at positions, connecting ports with edges,
    87→/// tapping nodes, custom node builders, grid snapping, and accessibility
    88→/// labelling.
    89→///
    90→/// {@category Composites}
    91→class OiFlowGraph extends StatefulWidget {
    92→  /// Creates an [OiFlowGraph].
    93→  const OiFlowGraph({
    94→    required this.nodes,
    95→    required this.edges,
    96→    required this.label,
    97→    this.nodeBuilder,
    98→    this.onNodeTap,
    99→    this.onNodeMove,
   100→    this.onEdgeCreate,
   101→    this.onEdgeDelete,
   102→    this.editable = true,
   103→    this.zoomable = true,
   104→    this.pannable = true,
   105→    this.snapToGrid = true,
   106→    this.gridSize = 20,
   107→    this.width,
   108→    this.height,
   109→    super.key,
   110→  });
   111→
   112→  /// The list of nodes to render.
   113→  final List<OiFlowNode> nodes;
   114→
   115→  /// The list of edges connecting node ports.
   116→  final List<OiFlowEdge> edges;
   117→
   118→  /// Accessibility label for the flow graph.
   119→  final String label;
   120→
   121→  /// An optional custom builder for rendering nodes.
   122→  ///
   123→  /// When null a default card representation is used.
   124→  final Widget Function(OiFlowNode)? nodeBuilder;
   125→
   126→  /// Called when a node is tapped.
   127→  final ValueChanged<OiFlowNode>? onNodeTap;
   128→
   129→  /// Called when a node is moved to a new position.
   130→  final void Function(OiFlowNode, Offset)? onNodeMove;
   131→
   132→  /// Called when a new edge is created between two ports.
   133→  final void Function(String sourcePort, String targetPort)? onEdgeCreate;
   134→
   135→  /// Called when an edge is deleted.
   136→  final ValueChanged<OiFlowEdge>? onEdgeDelete;
   137→
   138→  /// Whether the graph supports interactive editing (moving nodes, etc.).
   139→  final bool editable;
   140→
   141→  /// Whether the graph supports zooming.
   142→  final bool zoomable;
   143→
   144→  /// Whether the graph supports panning.
   145→  final bool pannable;
   146→
   147→  /// Whether nodes snap to the grid when moved.
   148→  final bool snapToGrid;
   149→
   150→  /// The grid cell size in logical pixels.
   151→  final double gridSize;
   152→
   153→  /// The width of the graph canvas.
   154→  final double? width;
   155→
   156→  /// The height of the graph canvas.
   157→  final double? height;
   158→
   159→  @override
   160→  State<OiFlowGraph> createState() => _OiFlowGraphState();
   161→}
   162→
   163→class _OiFlowGraphState extends State<OiFlowGraph> {
   164→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   165→  // Constants
   166→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   167→
   168→  static const double _defaultNodeWidth = 140;
   169→  static const double _defaultNodeHeight = 60;
   170→  static const double _portRadius = 5;
   171→
   172→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   173→  // Build
   174→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   175→
   176→  @override
   177→  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
   178→    final colors = context.colors;
   179→    final resolvedWidth = widget.width ?? 600;
   180→    final resolvedHeight = widget.height ?? 400;
   181→
   182→    // Map node keys for quick lookup.
   183→    final nodeMap = <Object, OiFlowNode>{};
   184→    for (final node in widget.nodes) {
   185→      nodeMap[node.key] = node;
   186→    }
   187→
   188→    return Semantics(
   189→      label: widget.label,
   190→      child: SizedBox(
   191→        width: resolvedWidth,
   192→        height: resolvedHeight,
   193→        child: Stack(
   194→          clipBehavior: Clip.none,
   195→          children: [
   196→            // Grid background.
   197→            if (widget.snapToGrid)
   198→              Positioned.fill(
   199→                child: CustomPaint(
   200→                  key: const ValueKey('oi_flow_graph_grid'),
   201→                  painter: _GridPainter(
   202→                    gridSize: widget.gridSize,
   203→                    color: colors.borderSubtle,
   204→                  ),
   205→                ),
   206→              ),
   207→            // Edges layer.
   208→            Positioned.fill(
   209→              child: CustomPaint(
   210→                painter: _EdgePainter(
   211→                  edges: widget.edges,
   212→                  nodeMap: nodeMap,
   213→                  nodeWidth: _defaultNodeWidth,
   214→                  nodeHeight: _defaultNodeHeight,
   215→                  defaultColor: colors.border,
   216→                  textColor: colors.textMuted,
   217→                ),
   218→              ),
   219→            ),
   220→            // Node layer.
   221→            for (final node in widget.nodes)
   222→              Positioned(
   223→                left: node.position.dx,
   224→                top: node.position.dy,
   225→                child: _buildNode(context, node, colors),
   226→              ),
   227→          ],
   228→        ),
   229→      ),
   230→    );
   231→  }
   232→
   233→  Widget _buildNode(
   234→    BuildContext context,
   235→    OiFlowNode node,
   236→    OiColorScheme colors,
   237→  ) {
   238→    Widget content;
   239→
   240→    if (widget.nodeBuilder != null) {
   241→      content = widget.nodeBuilder!(node);
   242→    } else {
   243→      content = Container(
   244→        width: _defaultNodeWidth,
   245→        height: _defaultNodeHeight,
   246→        decoration: BoxDecoration(
   247→          color: node.color ?? colors.surface,
   248→          border: Border.all(color: colors.border),
   249→          borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(8),
   250→        ),
   251→        child: Center(
   252→          child: Row(
   253→            mainAxisSize: MainAxisSize.min,
   254→            mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.center,
   255→            children: [
   256→              if (node.icon != null) ...[
   257→                Icon(node.icon, size: 16, color: colors.text),
   258→                const SizedBox(width: 6),
   259→              ],
   260→              Flexible(
   261→                child: Text(
   262→                  node.label,
   263→                  style: TextStyle(
   264→                    color: colors.text,
   265→                    fontSize: 13,
   266→                    fontWeight: FontWeight.w600,
   267→                  ),
   268→                  overflow: TextOverflow.ellipsis,
   269→                ),
   270→              ),
   271→            ],
   272→          ),
   273→        ),
   274→      );
   275→    }
   276→
   277→    // Port indicators.
   278→    final portIndicators = <Widget>[];
   279→    for (var i = 0; i < node.inputs.length; i++) {
   280→      portIndicators.add(
   281→        Positioned(
   282→          left: -_portRadius,
   283→          top:
   284→              _defaultNodeHeight * (i + 1) / (node.inputs.length + 1) -
   285→              _portRadius,
   286→          child: _PortDot(
   287→            key: ValueKey('oi_flow_port_in_${node.key}_${node.inputs[i]}'),
   288→            color: colors.primary.base,
   289→          ),
   290→        ),
   291→      );
   292→    }
   293→    for (var i = 0; i < node.outputs.length; i++) {
   294→      portIndicators.add(
   295→        Positioned(
   296→          right: -_portRadius,
   297→          top:
   298→              _defaultNodeHeight * (i + 1) / (node.outputs.length + 1) -
   299→              _portRadius,
   300→          child: _PortDot(
   301→            key: ValueKey('oi_flow_port_out_${node.key}_${node.outputs[i]}'),
   302→            color: colors.primary.base,
   303→          ),
   304→        ),
   305→      );
   306→    }
   307→
   308→    Widget nodeWidget = SizedBox(
   309→      key: ValueKey('oi_flow_node_${node.key}'),
   310→      width: _defaultNodeWidth,
   311→      height: _defaultNodeHeight,
   312→      child: Stack(
   313→        clipBehavior: Clip.none,
   314→        children: [content, ...portIndicators],
   315→      ),
   316→    );
   317→
   318→    if (widget.onNodeTap != null) {
   319→      nodeWidget = GestureDetector(
   320→        onTap: () => widget.onNodeTap!(node),
   321→        child: nodeWidget,
   322→      );
   323→    }
   324→
   325→    if (widget.editable && widget.onNodeMove != null) {
   326→      nodeWidget = GestureDetector(
   327→        onPanUpdate: (details) {
   328→          var newPos = node.position + details.delta;
   329→          if (widget.snapToGrid) {
   330→            newPos = Offset(
   331→              (newPos.dx / widget.gridSize).round() * widget.gridSize,
   332→              (newPos.dy / widget.gridSize).round() * widget.gridSize,
   333→            );
   334→          }
   335→          widget.onNodeMove!(node, newPos);
   336→        },
   337→        child: nodeWidget,
   338→      );
   339→    }
   340→
   341→    return nodeWidget;
   342→  }
   343→}
   344→
   345→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   346→// Port dot widget
   347→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   348→
   349→/// A small circular indicator for a node port.
   350→class _PortDot extends StatelessWidget {
   351→  const _PortDot({required this.color, super.key});
   352→
   353→  final Color color;
   354→
   355→  @override
   356→  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
   357→    return Container(
   358→      width: 10,
   359→      height: 10,
   360→      decoration: BoxDecoration(color: color, shape: BoxShape.circle),
   361→    );
   362→  }
   363→}
   364→
   365→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   366→// Grid painter
   367→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   368→
   369→/// Paints a dot grid background for the flow graph canvas.
   370→class _GridPainter extends CustomPainter {
   371→  const _GridPainter({required this.gridSize, required this.color});
   372→
   373→  /// The distance between grid dots.
   374→  final double gridSize;
   375→
   376→  /// The color of grid dots.
   377→  final Color color;
   378→
   379→  @override
   380→  void paint(Canvas canvas, Size size) {
   381→    final paint = Paint()
   382→      ..color = color
   383→      ..strokeWidth = 1;
   384→
   385→    for (var x = 0.0; x < size.width; x += gridSize) {
   386→      for (var y = 0.0; y < size.height; y += gridSize) {
   387→        canvas.drawCircle(Offset(x, y), 0.5, paint);
   388→      }
   389→    }
   390→  }
   391→
   392→  @override
   393→  bool shouldRepaint(_GridPainter oldDelegate) =>
   394→      oldDelegate.gridSize != gridSize || oldDelegate.color != color;
   395→}
   396→
   397→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   398→// Edge painter
   399→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   400→
   401→/// Paints edges between node ports as bezier curves with arrowheads.
   402→class _EdgePainter extends CustomPainter {
   403→  const _EdgePainter({
   404→    required this.edges,
   405→    required this.nodeMap,
   406→    required this.nodeWidth,
   407→    required this.nodeHeight,
   408→    required this.defaultColor,
   409→    required this.textColor,
   410→  });
   411→
   412→  /// The list of edges to paint.
   413→  final List<OiFlowEdge> edges;
   414→
   415→  /// A map from node key to [OiFlowNode].
   416→  final Map<Object, OiFlowNode> nodeMap;
   417→
   418→  /// The default node width.
   419→  final double nodeWidth;
   420→
   421→  /// The default node height.
   422→  final double nodeHeight;
   423→
   424→  /// The default edge color.
   425→  final Color defaultColor;
   426→
   427→  /// The color for edge labels.
   428→  final Color textColor;
   429→
   430→  @override
   431→  void paint(Canvas canvas, Size size) {
   432→    for (final edge in edges) {
   433→      final source = nodeMap[edge.sourceNode];
   434→      final target = nodeMap[edge.targetNode];
   435→      if (source == null || target == null) continue;
   436→
   437→      // Find the port index for positioning.
   438→      final sourcePortIdx = source.outputs.indexOf(edge.sourcePort);
   439→      final targetPortIdx = target.inputs.indexOf(edge.targetPort);
   440→      if (sourcePortIdx < 0 || targetPortIdx < 0) continue;
   441→
   442→      // Source port exits from the right side of the node.
   443→      final sourceY =
   444→          source.position.dy +
   445→          nodeHeight * (sourcePortIdx + 1) / (source.outputs.length + 1);
   446→      final sourceX = source.position.dx + nodeWidth;
   447→
   448→      // Target port enters from the left side of the node.
   449→      final targetY =
   450→          target.position.dy +
   451→          nodeHeight * (targetPortIdx + 1) / (target.inputs.length + 1);
   452→      final targetX = target.position.dx;
   453→
   454→      final start = Offset(sourceX, sourceY);
   455→      final end = Offset(targetX, targetY);
   456→
   457→      final paint = Paint()
   458→        ..color = defaultColor
   459→        ..strokeWidth = 1.5
   460→        ..style = PaintingStyle.stroke;
   461→
   462→      // Draw a cubic bezier curve.
   463→      final controlOffset = (end.dx - start.dx).abs() / 2;
   464→      final path = Path()
   465→        ..moveTo(start.dx, start.dy)
   466→        ..cubicTo(
   467→          start.dx + controlOffset,
   468→          start.dy,
   469→          end.dx - controlOffset,
   470→          end.dy,
   471→          end.dx,
   472→          end.dy,
   473→        );
   474→      canvas.drawPath(path, paint);
   475→
   476→      // Draw arrowhead.
   477→      const arrowLength = 8.0;
   478→      const arrowAngle = math.pi / 6;
   479→      final angle = math.atan2(end.dy - start.dy, end.dx - start.dx);
   480→
   481→      final headPaint = Paint()
   482→        ..color = defaultColor
   483→        ..style = PaintingStyle.fill;
   484→
   485→      final arrowPath = Path()
   486→        ..moveTo(end.dx, end.dy)
   487→        ..lineTo(
   488→          end.dx - arrowLength * math.cos(angle - arrowAngle),
   489→          end.dy - arrowLength * math.sin(angle - arrowAngle),
   490→        )
   491→        ..lineTo(
   492→          end.dx - arrowLength * math.cos(angle + arrowAngle),
   493→          end.dy - arrowLength * math.sin(angle + arrowAngle),
   494→        )
   495→        ..close();
   496→      canvas.drawPath(arrowPath, headPaint);
   497→
   498→      // Draw edge label if present.
   499→      if (edge.label != null) {
   500→        final midPoint = Offset(
   501→          (start.dx + end.dx) / 2,
   502→          (start.dy + end.dy) / 2 - 10,
   503→        );
   504→        final textPainter = TextPainter(
   505→          text: TextSpan(
   506→            text: edge.label,
   507→            style: TextStyle(color: textColor, fontSize: 11),
   508→          ),
   509→          textDirection: TextDirection.ltr,
   510→        )..layout();
   511→        textPainter.paint(
   512→          canvas,
   513→          midPoint - Offset(textPainter.width / 2, textPainter.height / 2),
   514→        );
   515→      }
   516→    }
   517→  }
   518→
   519→  @override
   520→  bool shouldRepaint(_EdgePainter oldDelegate) => true;
   521→}

1→import 'package:flutter/widgets.dart';
     2→import 'package:obers_ui/src/foundation/theme/oi_theme.dart';
     3→import 'package:obers_ui/src/primitives/interaction/oi_tappable.dart';
     4→
     5→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
     6→// Supporting types
     7→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
     8→
     9→/// A column in an [OiKanban] board.
    10→///
    11→/// Each column has a unique [key], a [title], a list of [items], and an
    12→/// optional [color] accent applied to the column header.
    13→@immutable
    14→class OiKanbanColumn<T> {
    15→  /// Creates an [OiKanbanColumn].
    16→  const OiKanbanColumn({
    17→    required this.key,
    18→    required this.title,
    19→    required this.items,
    20→    this.color,
    21→  });
    22→
    23→  /// Unique identifier for this column.
    24→  final Object key;
    25→
    26→  /// Title displayed in the column header.
    27→  final String title;
    28→
    29→  /// The cards contained in this column.
    30→  final List<T> items;
    31→
    32→  /// Optional accent colour for the column header.
    33→  final Color? color;
    34→}
    35→
    36→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    37→// OiKanban
    38→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    39→
    40→/// A Kanban board with draggable cards across columns.
    41→///
    42→/// Supports drag-and-drop between columns, column reordering, WIP limits,
    43→/// collapsible columns, and quick card editing.
    44→///
    45→/// {@category Modules}
    46→class OiKanban<T> extends StatefulWidget {
    47→  /// Creates an [OiKanban].
    48→  const OiKanban({
    49→    super.key,
    50→    required this.columns,
    51→    required this.label,
    52→    this.onCardMove,
    53→    this.onColumnReorder,
    54→    this.cardBuilder,
    55→    this.columnHeader,
    56→    this.reorderColumns = true,
    57→    this.wipLimits,
    58→    this.quickEdit = true,
    59→    this.collapsibleColumns = true,
    60→    this.addColumn = false,
    61→    this.onAddColumn,
    62→    this.cardKey,
    63→  });
    64→
    65→  /// The columns displayed on the board.
    66→  final List<OiKanbanColumn<T>> columns;
    67→
    68→  /// Accessible label for the kanban board.
    69→  final String label;
    70→
    71→  /// Called when a card is moved between columns.
    72→  final void Function(T item, Object fromColumn, Object toColumn, int newIndex)?
    73→  onCardMove;
    74→
    75→  /// Called when columns are reordered.
    76→  final void Function(int oldIndex, int newIndex)? onColumnReorder;
    77→
    78→  /// Custom builder for card widgets. Falls back to [toString] in a card.
    79→  final Widget Function(T item)? cardBuilder;
    80→
    81→  /// Custom builder for column header widgets.
    82→  final Widget Function(OiKanbanColumn<T>)? columnHeader;
    83→
    84→  /// Whether columns may be reordered by the user.
    85→  final bool reorderColumns;
    86→
    87→  /// Work-in-progress limits per column, keyed by [OiKanbanColumn.key].
    88→  final Map<Object, int>? wipLimits;
    89→
    90→  /// Whether quick-edit mode is enabled.
    91→  final bool quickEdit;
    92→
    93→  /// Whether columns can be collapsed.
    94→  final bool collapsibleColumns;
    95→
    96→  /// Whether to show an "Add column" button at the end.
    97→  final bool addColumn;
    98→
    99→  /// Called when the user taps the "Add column" button.
   100→  final VoidCallback? onAddColumn;
   101→
   102→  /// Extracts a unique key from each card item.
   103→  final Object Function(T)? cardKey;
   104→
   105→  @override
   106→  State<OiKanban<T>> createState() => _OiKanbanState<T>();
   107→}
   108→
   109→class _OiKanbanState<T> extends State<OiKanban<T>> {
   110→  final Set<Object> _collapsedColumns = {};
   111→
   112→  void _toggleCollapse(Object columnKey) {
   113→    setState(() {
   114→      if (_collapsedColumns.contains(columnKey)) {
   115→        _collapsedColumns.remove(columnKey);
   116→      } else {
   117→        _collapsedColumns.add(columnKey);
   118→      }
   119→    });
   120→  }
   121→
   122→  bool _isOverWipLimit(OiKanbanColumn<T> column) {
   123→    if (widget.wipLimits == null) return false;
   124→    final limit = widget.wipLimits![column.key];
   125→    if (limit == null) return false;
   126→    return column.items.length > limit;
   127→  }
   128→
   129→  @override
   130→  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
   131→    return Semantics(
   132→      label: widget.label,
   133→      container: true,
   134→      child: SingleChildScrollView(
   135→        scrollDirection: Axis.horizontal,
   136→        child: Row(
   137→          crossAxisAlignment: CrossAxisAlignment.start,
   138→          children: [
   139→            for (final column in widget.columns) _buildColumn(context, column),
   140→            if (widget.addColumn) _buildAddColumnButton(context),
   141→          ],
   142→        ),
   143→      ),
   144→    );
   145→  }
   146→
   147→  Widget _buildColumn(BuildContext context, OiKanbanColumn<T> column) {
   148→    final colors = context.colors;
   149→    final collapsed = _collapsedColumns.contains(column.key);
   150→    final overWip = _isOverWipLimit(column);
   151→
   152→    return Container(
   153→      width: 280,
   154→      margin: const EdgeInsets.all(8),
   155→      decoration: BoxDecoration(
   156→        color: colors.surface,
   157→        borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(8),
   158→        border: Border.all(
   159→          color: overWip ? colors.error.base : colors.borderSubtle,
   160→        ),
   161→      ),
   162→      child: Column(
   163→        mainAxisSize: MainAxisSize.min,
   164→        crossAxisAlignment: CrossAxisAlignment.stretch,
   165→        children: [
   166→          _buildColumnHeader(context, column, collapsed, overWip),
   167→          if (!collapsed) _buildColumnBody(context, column),
   168→        ],
   169→      ),
   170→    );
   171→  }
   172→
   173→  Widget _buildColumnHeader(
   174→    BuildContext context,
   175→    OiKanbanColumn<T> column,
   176→    bool collapsed,
   177→    bool overWip,
   178→  ) {
   179→    final colors = context.colors;
   180→
   181→    if (widget.columnHeader != null) {
   182→      return widget.columnHeader!(column);
   183→    }
   184→
   185→    final wipLimit = widget.wipLimits?[column.key];
   186→
   187→    return OiTappable(
   188→      onTap: widget.collapsibleColumns
   189→          ? () => _toggleCollapse(column.key)
   190→          : null,
   191→      child: Container(
   192→        padding: const EdgeInsets.symmetric(horizontal: 12, vertical: 10),
   193→        decoration: BoxDecoration(
   194→          color: column.color?.withValues(alpha: 0.1),
   195→          borderRadius: const BorderRadius.vertical(top: Radius.circular(8)),
   196→        ),
   197→        child: Row(
   198→          children: [
   199→            if (column.color != null)
   200→              Container(
   201→                width: 8,
   202→                height: 8,
   203→                margin: const EdgeInsets.only(right: 8),
   204→                decoration: BoxDecoration(
   205→                  color: column.color,
   206→                  shape: BoxShape.circle,
   207→                ),
   208→              ),
   209→            Expanded(
   210→              child: Text(
   211→                column.title,
   212→                style: TextStyle(
   213→                  fontSize: 14,
   214→                  fontWeight: FontWeight.w600,
   215→                  color: colors.text,
   216→                ),
   217→                maxLines: 1,
   218→                overflow: TextOverflow.ellipsis,
   219→              ),
   220→            ),
   221→            Text(
   222→              wipLimit != null
   223→                  ? '${column.items.length}/$wipLimit'
   224→                  : '${column.items.length}',
   225→              style: TextStyle(
   226→                fontSize: 12,
   227→                fontWeight: FontWeight.w400,
   228→                color: overWip ? colors.error.base : colors.textMuted,
   229→              ),
   230→            ),
   231→            if (widget.collapsibleColumns) ...[
   232→              const SizedBox(width: 4),
   233→              Icon(
   234→                collapsed
   235→                    ? const IconData(0xe5cf, fontFamily: 'MaterialIcons')
   236→                    : const IconData(0xe5ce, fontFamily: 'MaterialIcons'),
   237→                size: 16,
   238→                color: colors.textMuted,
   239→              ),
   240→            ],
   241→          ],
   242→        ),
   243→      ),
   244→    );
   245→  }
   246→
   247→  Widget _buildColumnBody(BuildContext context, OiKanbanColumn<T> column) {
   248→    final colors = context.colors;
   249→
   250→    if (column.items.isEmpty) {
   251→      return Padding(
   252→        padding: const EdgeInsets.all(16),
   253→        child: Text(
   254→          'No items',
   255→          textAlign: TextAlign.center,
   256→          style: TextStyle(fontSize: 13, color: colors.textMuted),
   257→        ),
   258→      );
   259→    }
   260→
   261→    return Padding(
   262→      padding: const EdgeInsets.all(8),
   263→      child: Column(
   264→        mainAxisSize: MainAxisSize.min,
   265→        children: [
   266→          for (var i = 0; i < column.items.length; i++)
   267→            Padding(
   268→              padding: EdgeInsets.only(top: i == 0 ? 0 : 6),
   269→              child: _buildCard(context, column.items[i]),
   270→            ),
   271→        ],
   272→      ),
   273→    );
   274→  }
   275→
   276→  Widget _buildCard(BuildContext context, T item) {
   277→    final colors = context.colors;
   278→
   279→    if (widget.cardBuilder != null) {
   280→      return widget.cardBuilder!(item);
   281→    }
   282→
   283→    return Container(
   284→      width: double.infinity,
   285→      padding: const EdgeInsets.all(10),
   286→      decoration: BoxDecoration(
   287→        color: colors.surface,
   288→        borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(6),
   289→        border: Border.all(color: colors.borderSubtle),
   290→      ),
   291→      child: Text(
   292→        item.toString(),
   293→        style: TextStyle(fontSize: 13, color: colors.text),
   294→      ),
   295→    );
   296→  }
   297→
   298→  Widget _buildAddColumnButton(BuildContext context) {
   299→    final colors = context.colors;
   300→
   301→    return Padding(
   302→      padding: const EdgeInsets.all(8),
   303→      child: OiTappable(
   304→        onTap: widget.onAddColumn,
   305→        child: Container(
   306→          width: 280,
   307→          padding: const EdgeInsets.symmetric(horizontal: 12, vertical: 10),
   308→          decoration: BoxDecoration(
   309→            borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(8),
   310→            border: Border.all(color: colors.borderSubtle),
   311→          ),
   312→          child: Row(
   313→            mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.center,
   314→            children: [
   315→              Icon(
   316→                const IconData(0xe145, fontFamily: 'MaterialIcons'),
   317→                size: 16,
   318→                color: colors.textMuted,
   319→              ),
   320→              const SizedBox(width: 6),
   321→              Text(
   322→                'Add column',
   323→                style: TextStyle(
   324→                  fontSize: 13,
   325→                  fontWeight: FontWeight.w500,
   326→                  color: colors.textMuted,
   327→                ),
   328→              ),
   329→            ],
   330→          ),
   331→        ),
   332→      ),
   333→    );
   334→  }
   335→}
